text: I love Starbucks. I am a regular customer, but I've always had problems with this location. But on this one instance my friend and I went in there and we were in a hurry. We were Almost the only ones in there on a Sunday night not even close to closing time. We ordered two drinks and sat there wondering why they're taking so long. Then when we are about as we saw they were sitting on the counter. The ice had melted and my friends drink and my drink was no longer warm. I was very frustrated because they could see that we were sitting there waiting for our drinks for a while. They never called our drinks or our names we would've heard because we were the only ones in there at that time. Very frustrating when they were all just standing there and you were waiting for drinks but didn't say anything which made my friend and I very late to our event.

text: At first glance, it's easy to miss this Starbucks driving down olive boulevard in Creve coeur. It is in an unassuming building, but directed right off the main road. There is plenty of parking as you pull in and the shop has two entrances. The staff was super efficient, friendly and helpful. This seems to be a necessity as the location was in the middle of their morning rush. They got my order as I was waiting in line, didn't mess anything up and had my coffee and pastry ready in lightning speed. Plenty of seating choices, with couches and tables, so I decided to do a little work. All in all, didn't expect such a big Starbucks in this building, but ended up enjoying my experience. I would definitely visit this location again, as I was able to get a lot of work done. All while enjoying a hot coffee with free refills!

text:
I'm a bit obsessed with Starbucks' cold brew as it is better than any coffee you can get anywhere else. All of the drink options are great, but a downfall is that they've gotten rid of their best syrup (Sugar Free Cinnamon Dolce). I also can't justify paying so much for a cup of coffee on the regular, but believe me -- when I get the craving, the cold brew is quite a treat!
I also really enjoy their iced chai latte, which has such great flavor! The chai they use is simply perfect. If you want a lower carb/sugar/calorie option , request a brewed chai with sugar free vanilla and half and half. It's just as good, but a bit healthier for those looking!
Finally; huge shout out to the crew at the greenwood Starbucks off of 31. It's super busy and they all are working quickly, efficiently, and with a smile!

text:
This is it. Today was the day that I gave Starbucks one more chance and they failed me. I really never buy iced coffee anymore. David gave me a very nice coffee maker, so I can make iced coffee at home, but I was in a pinch today so I thought I would stop in here and grab a quick cup. Big mistake. Huge. 

Time and time again they fill the entire cup with big chunks of ice and then put in the coffee. The result is a cup of coffee that I finish before the ice even melts. My grande was probably a quarter cup of actual coffee. Last time I asked them not to do this and they did it anyway. This time I totally forgot to mention it, but I feel as if it's common sense. 

Just for the record: I don't blame the employees- I know they are only doing as instructed. I blame Starbucks HQ for being stingy with their coffee. As much as they are charging, they should be willing to be a little more accommodating.

text: I previously gave this Starbucks a great review a few months ago and I'm sad to say my view of this Starbucks has totally changed. I've really been back and forth about changing my review but today pushed me a bit over the edge. I've been coming here a few times a week for the past year so I know what times are usually good to avoid a crowd. I work up the street so I usually give myself an extra 25 - 30 minutes to make sure I'm able to stop here because the drinks are great. However, the last few months the staff has changed and has become very unfriendly and slow. Not to mention, a customer in front of me ordered a drink with apple juice or something in it and the barista couldn't get the pouch open and opened it with HIS TEETH. I'm happy the customer demanded a new drink and for him to get scissors because if she didn't I would have. So unsanitary. He huffed and puffed about it too which I thought was ridiculous. Anyway, today I walked into Starbucks at 7:40am. My work is 2 minutes up the road so in my mind I had plenty of time. I walked in and saw two customers ahead of me so I figured it'd be a 5 minute wait at best. That wasn't the case at all. Every person working I've never seen before. Someone had to be new because I didn't even get to order my drink until 7:50. I ordered a sandwich and a vanilla latte macchiato. The cashier double checked everything which I was happy about because at this point I'm already irritated by the wait. Not to mention the line behind me is now outrageous, at 7:50am on a Saturday. No events or anything we're going on nearby. As I'm waiting 15 minutes I notice that people who ordered after me start getting their drinks. The barista has a drink sitting at the bar which was a caramel macchiato. I pick it up and see it's my order because the correct order is written on the cup, I explained to her it was not my order and she said she'd make another. No apology but I wasn't about to wait while the line kept growing. I just took the drink and left. Horrible service. Sorry to say I won't be back because when they can make a drink it's delicious. I left at 8:10. 30 minute wait for a coffee is OUTRAGEOUS

text: My fav coffee chain. Friendly staff. They are always super busy. The breakfast selection is great, my kids love the parfaits, I really like the cheese danish. It's clean and of course it has wifi. Very close to where we live so it's very convenient. My usual drink is always the mocha frapp with extra mocha and w/o whip. But my hubby said one of his co-worker, ordered a 'green' drink and she said it was green tea. He knew I like green tea so told me to try. I did try both the latte and the frapp. Surprisingly, I actually like the frapp over the latte. Oh, n it tastes great with the whip. Me, a no whip on my drink person actually likes the whip on the green tea frapp, lol! Now I have a 2nd favorite drink. I even got my hubby into the green tea frapp. :D Although, I do recommend that if u get the green tea latte, is to make sure n tell them to mix up the green tea powder throughly, if not it will be some on the bottom of ur cup, and it won't taste good.

text:
My wife and I submitted a mobile order after eating next door at PDQ. It was my birthday and if you have the Starbucks app installed, they give you a free birthday drink. We submitted our order and I was pleased to see that the birthday offer chose the most expensive drink on the order (and didn't really seem to care that I added some extras like mocha drizzle). Anyway, the app said our order would be ready in about ten minutes, at right at ten minutes, we were sitting in the drive through. The person in front of us had a long complicated order (FOR ONE DRINK) and we sat there listening to her chat with the Starbucks employee for about five minutes before finally pulling forward. When I got to the speaker, you could hear the sense of relief in the order taker's voice when I said "Hi there, mobile order for James."

Anyway, we pulled around, and quickly picked up our drinks. They were made exactly as we had specified and were absolutely delicious. Lemmie tell you, Starbucks has a winner with their cold brew lines. I love mine with a bit of sweet cream and some mocha drizzle and this particular location nailed it. 

Note, it may be easier to walk inside and pick up your order as the line for the drive through tends to wrap around in weird places and block traffic.

text:
Ah, the USF Tampa Library Starbucks...

You can't complain about the coffee - since they stand by the Starbucks promise of remaking your beverage if it's not up to standard. The food is standard: your danishes, bars, and prepackaged salads/sandwiches (which are a lifesaver, but not without a price: I cringe each time I recall the time I stumbled desperately into this Starbucks looking for something to eat after a 7 hour study session and wound up spending ten dollars on a tea and a cheese sandwich. True story). 

Study-wise? Well, you can't go wrong - and here's why:

- It's in the library. If you can't get your stuff done at Starbucks, please, move to one of the numerous other rooms/floors in the library. 

- That said, if you want to snag a seat in the Starbucks seating area - you better get there when it opens (don't know the hours? Just click the site link and it'll take you to the calendar of hours for the month) or during an off-peak hour (typically after lunch, when people tend to take most their classes). Sunday is always an option - it is a tomb in there on Sundays.

- I recommend this spot if you're doing some reading for a lit class. Starbucks is a social spot, and the air is always abuzz with conversation. I found it is far easier to ignore it when you're reading a book or a short story for class. It is not so easy when you're doing math, or reading an article for a political science class.

- Wifi is free, but only for USF students/faculty/staff.

text:
I come here quite often because it's the closest one to my work so it's always on the way.  Though I do go to a ton of different ones depending on the day.  This one is a smaller one in a weird looking building on the corner of Olive and Craig.  Its never been my favorite one, but, lately this location has really been getting on my nerves.  

The parking lot SUCKSSSS like major bad.  To get into it or out (so you can go L on Olive) from the main entrants there's a steep incline that you better watch out for or else you might scrape up your car.  Traffic blocks this entrance often too.  Once in the lot is ultra tiny.  If it's a busy time (which is 75% of the time since this one is so tiny) you have to fight for a spot or park at the bank and walk over... It's just annoying because the lot is stressful!! 

I typically get an iced coffee with sweetener and extra extra room for cream (as in I prefer a grande in a venti if they let me).  About 8/10 times the sweetener is forgotten.  I say I want it at least 3 times at the register and the cashier even reads back my order... Yet no sweetener.  If they're busy it can be 5 minutes before you get their attention and then they've been rude about fixing it.  Dude you screwed that up.  

One time they forgot and also put about 5 ice cubes in it.  When I finally got their attention to remake it I didn't see the girl put the sweetener in it so I asked to make sure.  She acted so pissed off that I asked, but, it's like do it right the first time.. Then I won't have to question you! 

Another time I asked before leaving the counter after the girl handed it to me.  Well, the girl handing it to me didn't even make my drink.  She looked confused, glanced at the cup and behind her and said yeah.  As I walked away I heard her ask the other girl did you put sweetener in it? Wtf man, you straight lied to me about knowing that you put it in there... At least say I'm not sure hold on and ask or something! Not cool. 

Even today the girl at the register was not very friendly about my request for a tall in a grande.  I get it, sometimes you're a stickler but if I ask politely tell me you can't do it. That's fine. Don't say ok, and in front of me as the other girl is grabbing the cup mutter no tall.  Ok, hello I'm right here... See me?? Yea, just tell me. I asked for it for a reason, I wanted extra extra room, not just extra and I even states such when I ordered.   

Lately the customer service hasn't been great.  I don't mind having to walk in cuz no drive thru, I don't mind fighting for a table, I don't always mind the parking lot.  But for crying out loud get my order right and if it's wrong don't be a bitch about it! Geeze. You're lucky it's my favorite iced coffee.

text:
What can I say? It's your average neighborhood Starbucks that fails to live up to the hype. I'm not sure what the obsession with this place is. I do come here occasionally because I am usually offered (by a friend for example) a free meal because this is what they enjoy, so I take it. If I had to pay the full price for this stuff, I'd never come here.

I'm sure most of what I'm going to say is going to apply to every Starbucks out there. This is given the fact that they hardly do any real work to make the food besides heat it up and for the drinks, follow a set recipe. The only thing that'll possibly differ from other Starbucks locations is that this one tends to burn any food that's heated up - consistently. You can see the burnt bottom of my ham and cheese croissant in the photo. Must just be a feature of the oven here.

Now, onto the stuff that applies to every Starbucks I've ever been to. If you want a popular item, you can't always expect it. There will be times that items have run out. I bought this food today at 10:00 am and this was the first time they've had a ham and cheese croissant in days. In fact, when I asked if they had ham and cheese croissants, the employee responded with "yes we have one ham and cheese croissant". I'm not sure if he was trying to ration the ever-popular ham and cheese croissants by only selling me one, or that he really only had one croissant left at 10:00 am after not having them for days. With other items, you will find that this applies to any Starbucks.

The second major thing to note is that you're paying for half filled cups of whatever drink you get. In my pictures below you can see how much of my green tea frappuccino was just ice that I threw down the sink - OVER HALF OF IT. This was despite the fact that I asked them to double blend the drink because I suspected that this would happen. It sucks that their frappuccino drinks are never actually blended well enough. I've had green tea milkshakes better than this frap from a mall booth, and I was actually able to finish drinking them because they weren't half ice. It's just another way that Starbucks tries to scam you out of your money, though.

A few other small things... the Bantam bagels are okay but are far more bread than cream cheese. Definitely dry and in need of more cream cheese flavor. However, this is the closest thing you'll come to a bagel and cream cheese at Starbucks in your morning rush, because Starbucks doesn't put the cream cheese on your bagels for you. Also, for all the money you're paying for their overpriced stuff, you still have to ask for napkins. They do not come standard no matter what you order. Weird... I thought my $20 drink, bagel, and croissant warranted a few napkins free of charge.

Also, at this location, I once ordered on the mobile app and the inventory wasn't updated, apparently. When I got there, they didn't actually have 3 of the items I ordered in stock. It was okay, though, because they made up for it with great customer service. They refunded me for those items that weren't in stock and still let me choose another item for free. I do have to admit that their customer service is usually on point.

text:
If I were rating this solely based on the amazing individuals that work at this location then believe me, you'd see the biggest 5 stars possible. These employees get hounded daily by huge lines of obnoxious students. I've seen them be berated, I've seen them when they're flustered, and I've seen the way a lot of jerks treat them. I absolutely love every employee here and hope that everyone else appreciates them. 

Since this location is at the UA, and on campus right by the library, it gets slammed throughout the day. There are times where there are lines that go 20 people out the door and the employees here continue to bust butt to get drink orders out. Unfortunately, the UA has set them up for failure by also having an advanced ordering app (not the regular Starbucks app--they don't do that at this location) where while there are 30 people in line, there are then 30 college students in classes also ordering drinks. So at certain times of the day the "in person" customers are getting angry because "tap and go" customers are getting drinks before the "in person" orders. But imagine how hard it is for these few employees to keep up with the 30 people in line and the 30+ mobile orders happening at the same time. Really, UA has set them up for failure because people get cranky and take it out on these poor baristas who are doing amazing work. Unfortunately, I have to deduct a star for the long, long wait times and for the fact that I think its a horrible business practice to have all of these different ordering systems slamming people. But again, still 5 stars for the employees! 

They don't let you redeem your stars for a free beverage here. That's frustrating. You can, however, still get happy hour deals and other perks here. I mainly earn my stars here and then cash them out at "regular" locations. 

What I --really-- hate about this location is how they nickle and dime you. I switch out the milk for soy or coconut in all of my beverages. The upcharge for this is fine, as I appreciate them offering soy, and I realize that costs them a bit more to make. I have zero issues with this. But what irks me is I used to order drinks with "no ice" and I'd have no issues. The ice makes things too cold for me to drink, but sometimes I want something that isn't hot either. "No ice" is a great way to get something closer to room temp. Again, I've ordered like this several times with no issues (and I've NEVER had an issue at any other location ever). But suddenly I'm told there is a 50 cent upcharge here for "no ice". They're actually trying to charge you to take ice out of your drink. I've just saved you some water, some ice, the power it took to make the ice, etc., and the coffee I'm purchasing is around $6-7 and I usually purchase here 3x a week, 2x a day (that's 6 drinks a week, a loyal customer). And you really, really want to charge someone to take ice out of a drink?!

This "new rule" comes and goes. Sometimes they have it. Other times they don't. But basically, they need to get over all of their extra charges that don't exist anywhere else. Seriously, this doesn't exist at any other "regular" or "franchise" location I've ever been to. At some point, you need to realize you're making plenty of money and you don't need to charge to remove ice. After all, I don't get a discount if I take whipped cream off of my drink...

Employees: 5 stars
Long waits and weird mysterious extra charges: 3 stars
Greed of this location: no stars

text: Okay....somedays I rully rully need a reliable and good cappucino. GUILTY.

Starbucks is Starbucks.  And while I am not a huge fan of their coffee (in my opinion, it always tastes a little burnt) and I find it a bit disconcerting that their pastries, sandwiches, and drinks taste the same all over the world (no local flavours), as far as huge chains go, they aren't as evil as the rest (their labour practices are better than most other multinational corporations, for example).

However, I prefer to frequent locally owned cafes for my java fix, so I have only been to this location once.  I found the layout to be awkward, with massive columns taking up huge amounts of space and larges shelves of merchandise taking up even more precious real estate.  The result is a very crowded seating area with incredibly few seats available.  I found it claustrophobic and awkward not a pleasant place to sit and enjoy a cup of coffee.  Perhaps in the summer months with the patio open it might be nicer.  But this is Edmonton.  And our patio time is quite limited.

I found the baristas to be pleasant and helpful.  And the food and beverages tasted exactly as I expected them to, like Starbucks.

text:
If you're in Midtown Nashville looking for a Starbucks, this is the place!  

I'm usually very disappointed by Starbucks service level, but I am extremely surprised by this store's management and staff.  Being an early riser, coffee isn't just part of my routine, it's a ritual and addiction.  Like every other SBUX, it's uniform: drip coffee, espresso, and bakery goods.  Where the difference lies is in the ability of the staff to recognize recurring visitors as well as to recognize new patrons and to make them feel at home.  This store has a really great staff!  I would swear that they actually enjoy being there.

The café is always clean, and it's not usually too difficult to find a chair.  There are several outdoor tables as well that overlook Centennial Park.  Parking right in front is usually available.  On-site security is nice to see.  Should you walk in to find a line to the door, don't fret!  They herd us through quickly.

I've visited all of the Starbucks in Nashville since arriving here several weeks ago, and this store is by far the best in this Yelper's opinion.

text:
Westfield has noooooo drive thru Starbucks so I was sooo excited to stumble upon this drive thru one close to my house. 
Look-i love Starbucks...I spend a ton of money at Starbucks. In return, all I ask is decent coffee and friendly service. I know it's gonna be busy. I know I'm gonna wait.... Throw me an apologetic smile or something once in awhile. 
So when I went over the weekend and waited 20 mins only to get completely dismissed by the barista when I asked for her help in figuring out why I was so waiting for croissants, I was thoroughly disappointed.  When I apologized for interrupting HER and kindly asked if she could help, she offered nothing and said to me "oh i don't do warming station, my shift supervisor does." And that was it, you can imagine my frustration.  I hate leaving a bad review but she didn't even yell for someone. Or point to someone. Or anything. Not cool. 
So when I did find my person, she said the ticket never printed then walked away. Then 30 seconds later came back with a bag of my food. She said it was accidentally left at the drive thru window. The end. It had been sitting there for awhile..and I'd been waiting for 20 mins. 
Frankly, I want to give a lower star but because I received excellent service from a kid the day before who normally worked at a different store... The Starbucks gets an average.. 

Do better. It isn't difficult to be cool.

text:
I have to update my review as I have had the same problem recur every single week and no changes have been made, despite my pointing it out each time. Ever since a new barista was put on bar, she continuously serves grande lattes barely 3/4 full.  I have been a regular customer of this location for years and every time she makes my drink, it feels like half of it is missing. In fact, she is the ONLY one who does this so I always know it was her, and when I arrive to pick up a mobile order, there she is - and there is my drink - and it looks and feels like someone already drank nearly half. 

Each time it has happened I've asked a different barista to help me (I really hate complaining, plus I'm a regular and if they're busy, it is a pain in the rear for everyone AND I end up late to work). The one time I asked her why the drink seemed so... "less than full", she rudely replied with an attitude "How high do you want it to be??" Seriously? I understand a quarter inch is standard to avoid burns but anyone can see (based on the images that I supplied) that she is filling it nearly two INCHES from the rim, and covering it up with whipped cream. 

I asked for a remake the first 3 times that it happened but I can't keep asking for her to get it right. Now I'm asking that management address this issue and either politely train her how to measure...OR take her OFF the bar. Until that happens, I'll be a regular of the Kennedy Starbucks. I am paying over $5.00 for the drink, not for half or 3/4 of a drink. At this point, Starbucks owes me about 5 free drinks or some partial refunds. If it feels less than full, then it probably is. And no, more whipped cream is not the answer.

text:
I feel almost silly writing a review for a chain restaurant, but the service here is just beyond amazing. This Starbucks is located on a very busy corner, and it is not unusual to find a line from the front-door all the way to the ordering counter. Despite this, and despite the fact that is only ONE store-front out of a chain of thousands (millions?), the staff has made it so that you feel like you're in your neighborhood coffee shop. They know the customers by name, and their orders by heart. I barely even have to speak upon entry-- they ask me if I'm getting my usual, and moments later it's delivered into my hand. 
I love this place, and they set the bar extremely high for not only every other Starbucks location, but for every coffee shop in general.

So.... I waited to visit this location a couple of times before writing this review. I visited Saturday 9.14.19, Sunday 9.15.19 and today 9.16.19. I read the reviews  about how slow the store runs, how long the lines were, etc. 

I love Starbucks and sometimes I feel that Yelpers give negative or bad reviews just because they have nothing better to do. (Most Yelpers who give bad reviews have lots of negative reviews on their page)

My first visit to this location was Saturday September 14, 2019. It was around 4pm and the store was crowded. There were at least SIX baristas behind the counter, but it just didn't seem like they were moving "fast enough" to accommodate the crowd of people wanting their Starbucks fix. 

This visit, I purchased: a Treinta Iced Passion Tango Tea Lemonade, a Tall Salted Caramel Mocha Frappuccino and two Protein Boxes (PB&J on Wheat|Smoked Turkey). The barista prepared my tea as a Tall as opposed to a Treinta. It was horrible and I waited for such a long time, I thought I was going to miss my bus to work.


My second visit was Sunday September 15, 2019. After Saturday's visit, I was in and out simply because I didn't purchase a drink. There was still a crowd of people to wade through.

My third visit today was in the morning before the mall even opened. I was one of two customers in the store. The barista looked as if he was tired and really didn't want to be there. I purchased two Cheese Danishes and a Tall Iced Passion Tango Tea Lemonade. It took FOREVER!!! I actually missed my bus to go home.

It seems like there is no set time of day that will get in|out quickly (even if your purchase isn't complicated). You may want to visit this location if you are strolling around the mall and have the time and patience. 


Also apparently this location is in the hiring process for a new store manager. Maybe they need someone to whip the employees into shape, elevate morale and create better customer service.

text: First time to this location, i never have a problem at Starbucks. Stopped at this one due to location and needed to get a mug for a gift. I was looking at the sale items, and noticed one of the travel mugs lids were broken and so I lifted it to take the defected item to the register so no one would touch it or something and the top fell slid off and (it was glass) shattered more on the ground. I picked up the pieces and took it to the barista at the register and she was about to charge me for it. Her name was Rita. I informed her that I wasn't trying to buy the defected/broken item and she told me "but you just broke it" I explained what Had happened and that I was simply bringing it to them so it wouldn't get touched. She rolled her eyes and proceeded to ring up my items and take my order. I purchased a few things, so I asked for a bag and she ignored me. I paid and was waiting for a bag and she still hadn't given one, so I asked again "may I have a bag for my items, please? If you don't have one that's ok." She rolled her eyes and grabbed a bag in a snatching motion and gave it to me to bag my items myself. I told her thank you and to have a happy new year and packaged my items at the pickup area so I could get out of her way so she could help other customers since she clearly didn't want to help me. I hope she will read this and it will help her change her attitude towards the guests that come to her location. I love Starbucks but she was the most unhappy barista I have ever met. Cashier/barista Rita I really hope your holiday season gets better and you can find happiness in your day.

Supervisors: Kathy, Stephanie, Josh, Chris, Ashlie

text:
I usually go to the Starbucks at 15th and Market due to convenience, but I'm going to start coming here more often since its just a 10 minute walk away.This is my second time at this location and I'm writing this review as I'm sitting here. Compared to other Starbucks in the area, this one is much more capacious and has many more seats and outlets. At other coffee shops, I constantly find myself searching for a place to sit, and this usually results in me failing and having to migrate to another coffee shop. I haven't had such experiences here yet. 

The spaciousness also allows me to think better and feel less claustrophobic, even when there are a lot of people around. This is a great place for studying and getting work done. With that being said, the wifi is also fast, reliable, and free (yay!). 

Lastly, service both times have been friendly and accommodating; Drinks were made properly. The only complaint I have is that both times that I've been here, there were loud customers who were really distracting while I was trying to get work done.

text:
Alright this time I had the pleasure of checking out the indoors. As usual, it was packed on this Sunday morning but the interior was deceptively larger than I expected - there was a good mix of seating indoors (large rectangular tables, small round cafe tables, and squishy sofas). The patio had 2-3 tables too and both have ADA options. 

The women's bathroom was OOS during my study session so I ended up using the men's which is identical to the women's and even has a changing table! Huzzah!

Two things I'm going to be nit picky about - there weren't that many accessible outlets, especially near the handicapped tables. The other was that the AC was on fulllll blast- so much that the window shades and their metal beady pulleys were flapping about (see video). I left before I got to finish by drink because despite sitting by a sunny window, wearing long pants and a puffer vest, I was still freezing my bum :( 

I'm sticking with a solid 3 here now that I've seen both the drive thru and inside cafe.

text:

$.50 extra. But, when taken into consideration with my overall experience, it certainly didn't help in making it a pleasant one. If that had been me, I'd have poured some of the tea into a water cup making room for the apple juice, but only bc the customer was standing there watching.

text:
I am not really a fan of Starbucks.  Unfortunately at this mall all they have for coffee is Starbucks.  I ordered a venti Cold Brew which I can tolerate.  Most of the coffee at Starbucks just tastes burnt to me but as I said the cold brew is ok.

My wife ordered a Carmel frapucino light.  However they did not have the sugar free Carmel.  It is very sad to me that Starbucks would ever be missing any of the needed ingredients for their signature drinks.  The first drink they made did not have any coffee at all.  I did not even know they would make a Frapucino with no coffee.  The guy behind the counter said that kids get it that way.  My wife said it was "gross."

In any case she settled on a mocha frapucino light which ended up tasting ok.

The people behind the counter were nice which is why I am giving 2 stars instead of 1.  

It is also overly busy here which explains the slow service.

The bottom line is that I will continue to get coffee here when I am at the Mall because they seem to have a monopoly which explains the high prices.

text:
*No drive through, the closest Starbucks with one is a few miles south at 10002 N Dale Mabry Hwy.

I frequent this Starbucks to sit and get work done. The staff is always friendly. It may take a bit to get a table inside on which to work, they only have six tables: 2 four-seater high tops, 2 four-seater regular height tables and 2 two-seater regular height tables (I'm here right now lol.). They have outdoor seating on two sides of the building and all of it is under an awning.

I also put in a fair amount of orders here through the mobile app. Unfortunately they don't have a drive through and since this location is in a strip plaza parking may be a tad challenging if you're trying to just run in and grab a mobile order drink from the designated counter area for them. It's not that bad though.

The bathrooms have been clean here every time I've used them. Well, the mens'. Can't speak for the womens' (would I tell you if I could?).

Overall a solid 4/5 Starbucks. A drive through would probably make it 5/5 but short of a location change it ain't gonna happen.

There is a special place in hell for the guy or gal that designed or allowed this nightmare to exist.  Auto body shops dream of having this store near them. text:
This Starbucks is in a prime location--complete with a shared outdoor courtyard and some good, adjacent restaurants. The inside is also very spacious and welcoming. I always go here assuming it's a great place to study and have a delicious cup of coffee. On both counts it misses the mark. Not a good Starbucks but also not a bad Starbucks.

The cleanliness is my main issue. I thought all Starbucks tried to maintain a well-kept environment for their customers? I figured this was standard procedure. Somehow this place is just dirty and unkept every single time I visit. The bathroom was so disgusting I literally walked in and walked out. This is really unacceptable, in my opinion. There are much better-kept local coffee shops right across the street (The French Press) that are crowded but manage to keep their space spotless. 

As for the coffee, the blended and espresso drinks are all overly-sweet. I have sometimes not been able to finish them because of this. On the bright side, they have a Clover Machine (for the black coffee lovers out there)! You can get a personally-crafted cup of any of their standard or reserve beans. This is something I highly recommend! 

Lastly, the employees are all really friendly! They always ask about my day and make a point to be cordial, even when they are swamped with orders.

text:
This review starts out at an unlikely place - Panera. Popped in there for lunch. My girlfriend just moved in so we are without wifi until Friday and I figured I would eat and cruise the web.

Had lunch and a coffee that was fair. At least until they kicked me off the internet after only ONE HOUR!

Never in all the years I have gone to coffee shops/sandwich shops that offer free internet was I KICKED OFF because you only get one hour of surfing! Are you kidding me????? First of all, I purchased lunch AND coffee. Secondly, the place easily had 35-40% open tables. 

I decided to go one block away to the Starbucks. Yea, thats right. Starbucks is one block away on 1081 West Chester Pike in West Chester, PA 19382. Got a nice snack pack of fruit and cheese. Was greeted by the very awesome Kim who assured me they love my business and would NEVER kick me off the internet unless it wasn't working. Had a bottle of water. 

Never again Panera. Hello Starbucks! They won me over!

text:
So we have come to know and expect a standard level of service from Starbucks no matter where we are in the world... unfortunately this one does not meet the standard. As this is a good location to meet up with friends (somewhat in the middle between north side Edmonton and St. Albert), I frequent it about once a month. Every single time there has been some level of let down... either the baristas plain ignore me standing at the counter (for a full 6 min), or they don't listen close enough to my order and get it wrong (twice), to the bathrooms not clean on a regular basis, and the cramped feeling inside. Generally I find the employees disrespectful towards patrons and I have even had the manager make me feel uncomfortable in a situation.

Inside is small and there are not many tables, the decor is standard, and the lighting is dim. The one bright spot in this review is that I have never had issues with the drive thru. Overall I would not recommend this location for your Starbucks fix unless absolutely necessary. And definitely not a good place for studying or relaxing due to the cramped space and lack of lighting.

text:
I was on my way to Hershey and came across the Peter J. Camiel rest stop. Once inside, I looked around at the options, which included PZA, Roy Rogers and Starbucks. After some deliberation, I decided to stop off at Starbucks. 

Unlike other rest stop Starbucks, this one has a fast moving line and full service including hot drinks and the usual Starbucks snacks. Even better, the line moved pretty fast! 

Eventually, I got up to the front of the line and placed my order. I didn't get anything too large, just a large hot chocolate and a muffin. 

While the cashier was nice, she was a bit too personable at times. If there were more people in the line, I can see that causing a longline.

My hot chocolate came out fast, though it was a bit watery in my opinion. 

When it comes to seating, you just have to sit in the rest stop, though they have a free phone charging station.

text:
I am a Starbucks addict and have a quad venti toffee nut soy latte every day at my local store in Ardmore. But when I am in center city on Saturdays to drop off my daughter at ballet, I get my fix at this Walnut Street location with my toddler before we head to the Barnes & Noble. I've probably been to this location thirty times, at least. 

It has never been a favorite location, given that it's often difficult to find parking (no fault of Starbucks, of course), but mostly because it's just kinda gross inside, and the seating is really awkward. Over the past year or so, they've played around with the seating arrangement (and lamentably got rid of two chaise lounges), but there doesn't seem to be a permanent way to arrange the tables so that people aren't blasted with cold air every time one of the two doors is opened. But, even the odd seating and grody interior aren't enough to turn me off from returning. My visit last week, however, may have been:

I ordered my drink and went to find a table with my toddler. A few minutes later I returned to the waiting area and saw what I thought might be my drink, but the woman who was in line after me had ordered several drinks, and she picked it up and added it to her collection. Then the barista called out some drink that wasn't mine, and the woman realized she had taken the wrong drink--mine--and apologized. Since she had already added sugar to the drink, she couldn't just give it me. Instead of the barista apologizing for the mix up, he mumbled "That's why you shouldn't walk away." Yes, he was talking to me, but he wasn't talking to me. He was bitching about me. If I hadn't had my toddler with me, I would have let him have it. But I held my tongue, and he he sighed as he started to remake my drink. As I watched him, I noticed that he made it with nonfat milk, not soy as I had ordered. But I didn't say anything because I didn't want to be presumptive; maybe he was making someone else's drink. But then he finished the drink and handed it to me, and I asked, "I'm sorry, does this have soy?" "No, I didn't think it had soy" he said. Yes, sorry, it does. Yup, he had to remake it AGAIN. And was he once remiss, apologetic or sorry? No, he was annoyed. I'm usually the first to give people credit when it's due, but this was probably the WORST barista I'd ever encountered. Not because he was incompetent, but because he was a jerk. Even if I had been a high-maintenance, hard-to-please, wrong customer, he should have put on a happy face, apologized for mixing up the drinks the first time, then apologized again for making my drink wrong the second time. And, he should have given me a free drink coupon. By the time I finally got my correct drink, it had been 20 minutes since I had ordered.  In most other Starbucks stores, the barista would have profusely apologized (and offered me a coupon). But nope, only in center city is Starbucks' customer service nonexistent.

text:
Came in one morning and ordered a venti blackberries and cream frappachino and a morning bun. Cashier rang me up and never gave me my morning bun. Got back in line to buy a bag of popcorn and get my pastry and I get an "oh you're back" I told her she charged me for my pastry but never gave it to me and all i got was a snarky look. 

After she all but through my pastry at me I waited at the end of the bar for my drink and started to notice that some of the people behind me in line were getting their drinks before me. While it's one thing if they were getting a regular cup of coffee it's another when they're getting frappuccinos and lattes. After 15-20 minutes of waiting one of the baristas came up to me and asked me what I ordered and if I was still waiting so I told her and apparently the cashier never marked my cup. The barista then made my drink and was very apologetic about it. 

I also try to help the cashier with the marking on one of the pastry bags because nor she or the manager could figure out what it was and being an employee at a real Starbucks I tried to help since I knew the marking and I all I got was a disgusted look from both the cashier and manager. 

Not to sound superior or anything buy If any of these things happened at my store it would not be tolerated at all. 

Since I'm on such a tight schedule in the morning I don't have enough time to go to the one in town or else I would.

text:
I think it's hard to review chain restaurants like Starbucks because they have so many company-specific standards to meet that you really should have a similar experience there no matter what location you go to. My visit to this location was exactly like any other Starbucks I've been to, except for the wifi. 

I was greeted when I walked in, the baristas were friendly, I got my drink quickly, and I was very happy with my chai tea late. 

However, I planned on spending a couple hours at Starbucks--I had a lot of school work to do and I drove several miles from my house to come to this location. But it totally wasn't worth the trip because the wifi was incredibly slow. When I was watching my lectures, they kept stopping and needing to buffer every 5 minutes. I've never had this problem anywhere else. The slow wifi was so annoying that I left early and went down the street to Corner Bakery Cafe--where I was able to watch my lectures without a single problem. Definitely won't be coming back to this Starbucks to study again.

text:
First Starbucks app order for my daughter today and all went wrong, then got worse. She ordered the Mango Dragonfruit Refresher with strawberry added (realized she ordered that extra once I returned home). I went to pick it up for her, because I'm a good Dad, and was handed a "clear-ish" looking drink with no explanation. So I asked, "isn't it supposed to be kinda pink?" Was told they're out of Dragonfruit but it wouldn't change the flavor at all. Here's from their website...

"This tropical-inspired pick-me-up is crafted with a refreshing combination of sweet mango and dragonfruit flavors, handshaken with ice and a scoop of real diced dragon fruit."

Turns out that dragonfruit must provide some dragonfruit flavor!! Called them once I got home about the missing strawberries and another worker told me again, after saying I wasn't charged for extra strawberries when my receipt clearly showed I was, that it didn't matter about the dragonfruit and if I drove back to the store she'd refund my 50 cents. That's not the way to correct this. The right answer would've been to refund it through the app or take my name for a future credit, not have me drive back to get a refund of 50 cents because you forgot the strawberry in my daughter's dragonfruit drink...minus the dragonfruit!!! 

We'll go elsewhere.

text:
Sunday morning scenario:

Line snaked through Sbux into the Marriott lobby. One barista on register. Two baristas on coffee and food. One barista doing nothing. One barista at the espresso, etc. area.

I patiently waited my turn and ordered the same thing I have ordered every day since 8/28; a Venti non-fat, no whip  pumpkin spice latte and picked up a Mango Juice from the fridge. I did not have enough on my Gold Card so I also handed the cashier $10 and asked her to put it on my card first. She condescendingly "explained" she could do it all at the same time. She put it on my card and rang my order and gave me some coin change?? (still haven't figured that one out).

I patiently waited FOREVER and watched as several people who came after me got their drinks. I politely asked the barista if he had a Venti Pumpkin Spice Latte coming up. He did not. Meanwhile the register girl yells down that I had ordered a Grande. I pulled out my receipt to verify that I had indeed ordered a Venti.

He finally made my drink (with whip and most likely Vitamin D milk) and gave me a card for a free drink next time.

I get that people make mistakes, but register girl seriously needs to not be in the customer service position of ringing customers. (There were other snide remarks heard while waiting there that I won't even go into.)

I love Sbux and like I said, I visit my local spot at least once per day. Not even remotely impressed with the management of this local.

Go to this Starbucks first. Trust me.

I place an online order. It was a lunch box, so there wasn't much to do but have it ready. I went there and one of the girls told me your order isn't ready yet, so I told her it's just a lunch box, so she said oooh ok go ahead and grab it. 

They didn't have the box I ordered, so I chose a different one, and the cashier charged me $3 for it. I was so upset because I used my rewards to pay for the previous box, so I felt deceived and also pay extra for a $6 box. I basically end up paying $9. 

I am upset not just for the poor customer service but for the extra fee. She acted like if I was trying to steal it. 

The first girl I understand she was busy making the drinks, so she didn't bother me at all. I mean I can grab a box. However, the cashier displayed such poor customer service. She didn't even attempted to look in the back to make sure they didn't have the other lunch box. She also wasn't even busy. I wasted in line to have her full attention. 

Just upsetting. Also, the bread is old, and the box has bacon which I don't eat, that is why I ordered the egg box. $9 to eat half of an apple. Great!

text:
So I'm not a huge fan of reviewing super big chains like Starbucks, because let's face it: regardless of if you have a negative experience you are probably going to continue going back for your over priced latte. At least I do. That being said, this Starbucks is special and I feel that it needs some love on Yelp. So this is me giving the Cool Springs Starbucks some love! 

I am working on my MBA right now, why is this important? Well, I have an endless amount of work that never is finished. This Starbucks is the perfect location for a halfway spot for all the members in my cohort. We are at this place 4+ times a week. This location is PERFECT for group work. Towards the back of the shop there is a huge long table that can fit like 14 people. It is an ideal spot for any study group or group of people wanting to work on something.

The staff is extremely friendly. I mean so great. I have had such cool conversations with so many of them, and they remember us. It's like a small town coffee shop feel as a huge chain. I have heard that this is the busiest location, but the line backs up outside primarily. Not inside.

Overall, if you live in the area and need a place to work, I recommend this location 110%.

text:
The only reason I ever come to this place at all is its proximity to my dorm and its prime spot as a late-night place to study, even as late as 2 am! These redeeming qualities are the only reason this Starbucks gets two stars instead of one.

The staff here are INCREDIBLY rude and hassle you in order to get your order and get the line moving as quickly as possible. This would normally be fine but the attitudes of almost every worker here are, simply put, awful. Over the times I've studied here, I've witnessed an employee here shout at a student and another employee roll their eyes at another customer who didn't know the difference between two kinds of coffee.

I guess this kind of behavior is acceptable when your customer base is a revolving door of students who come to you for caffeine out of necessity rather than free will.

Also, they charge $1 for a cup of water. Seriously? This is Starbucks, where their profit margins are wider than Hey Arnold's head; charging for a water is the epitome of cheap in my eyes - especially in this industry!

Save your time and go to another cafe if you can -- there are plenty of other options in the area. And if you insist on Starbucks go to the one at 34th and Walnut: the service is much, much better.

text:
Thank you Starbucks! You saved the day!
 I went to this location twice this morning, in the odd snowy conditions on this 11th of March. I ordered the wrong drink my wife asked me to go get her. She was not happy at all. So I went back to order another drink (the right one this time) and I told them I screwed up the order and needed another drink. (Grande White Mocha Frapp no whip) When I got to the window the Head Barista ,(Mgr.) ,asked me if they made the mistake or did I. I told them ot was totally my mistake and he told me he appreciated my brutally honest admission and rewarded me with the drink for no charge. I am forever greatful and will continue being a loyal customer. Not because I was given a free drink but because that's damn good customer service showing appreciation for my business, and that is in short supply in todays world. Than you again! 
                 Sincerely Greatful Patron!,
                              Justin G.

text: I am a Starbucks fan, even though I usually purchase traditional coffee. There's many different locales better than this one. text:
It's kind of rare for casinos to have a place like this where you can get an inexpensive coffee and sit in some comfy chairs and just take an extended break. Usually it's all about pushing you from machines/tables to an overpriced restaurant to the bar and they don't want you doing anything else there that doesn't involve money non-stop coming out of your pocket. So this Starbucks, which is like a typical one of their smaller locations with plenty of different types of seating, is a pleasant oddity.

Pricing is a bit higher than other locations in the area, but not much, about 20 cents more per drink. Open to midnight Friday and Saturday, I think 8 PM all other days.

Only issue is that there can be a monster line, but an hour or two after checkout time the place really mellows out. Well, that and they could be a bit more pro-active about cleaning the place up. Last visit they had 3 baristas chatting behind the counter with no line while there were old discarded coffee cups and casino drinks all over the tables.

text:
I'm the type of customer whose drink is unique (I know, I know) and memorized by a couple of the veteran baristas. "The usual?" they ask when I stroll in or roll up to the drive-thru speaker. I love my veteran baristas at this Starbucks. I even went on a date with one once! (Whaaaaat?)

And I've been coming to this particular location for the better part of two years, so I don't really feel bad about the rating I'm bestowing upon it today.

It seems that they've recently hired a slew of new employees who a) have no experience working with coffee and b) fail to listen to your drink order in the drive-thru line, resulting in having to repeat it 3-4 times. 

So what happens in this instance? Well, it seems this Starbucks is keen on training said employees during their rush: when people need to get the f%#@ to work. So the long (LONG) line that runs all the way into the traffic on Girls School Road (literally) remains at a standstill for a solid 20 minutes. 

A solid 20 minutes of waiting, followed by a new barista that either gets your order wrong repeatedly or just pauses, followed by ".... Um... What...?" in the most monotone I-don't-care voice I've ever heard. 

Look, I understand new employees need to be trained on making drinks and serving food as well as taking orders in the store and in the drive-thru line. But maybe rethink your plan of action and have the new guys MAKE the drinks during rush and TAKE the orders during the slower hours.

Until they get situated, I'll unfortunately be taking my business to the Starbucks further west in Avon. Sad face.

text:
I wanted to get a quick drink and snack with a friend of mine today and I decided to go to the Starbucks right on City Line Avenue, but it was closed. However, close by was this Starbucks that was located in Target so we decided to stop off. 

We were pleasantly surprised to see that this Starbucks had been expanded and improved as part of this Target's renovations. However, we were very disappointed. 

On the overhead menu was an advertisement for their Mango Dragonfruit Starbucks Refresher. However, when I asked the cashier what that is she just describe it as a "refresher." That response just shows how little she cared about helping me or other customers. She later described it as a "juice", but upon researching it the drink is actually caffeinated. I think Starbucks should also train their staff better. 

My friend who I was with ordered a dark roast, but was actually given a pike roast. He was even more disappointed by how customer service handled the situation. The staff member did not apologize once for the issue and acted as if she was doing him a favor by remaking his coffee. 

I also got a chocolate chip cookie, which there were no problems with. 

Altogether, I would avoid going to this Starbucks at all costs.

text:
Some of those drinks aren't coffee - they are sundaes!

I kinda get the whole Starbucks appeal, I guess. My daughter and son-in-law both used to be baristas there and she has tried to explain and justify it to me. I mean, I understand the addiction to caffeine and all. It's just that, for me personally - with a limited budget or "disposable income" as it is now phrased - I cannot see spending $5.00 for a cup of coffee - or the hint of coffee after all those flavors, creams and toppings are added - that's a sundae, that's not coffee.

So, while you are at Starbucks ordering your Yeti Carnival Margarita with vanilla syrup and a lemon slice or whatever - I'm down the road at McDonalds drive-thru fork-lifting a Big Breakfast into the trunk of my car, for about the same money - "Could I have that with a double stack of hotcakes, four syrups and extra grease on the bacon, please?" Plus, the coffee is basically FREE! And - about 10:00am while the top button on my jeans is still open - you're already snacking on the carrot sticks and tuna salad you were suppose to have for lunch...

text:
I've been into this Starbucks once before, but only briefly. I'm impressed by the space as it has a high ceiling, lots of windows and decent sized tables. It's not a residential area, so it seems like most of the patrons drive here. It's a well laid out, comfortable place.

Busyness - For the middle of the afternoon it's fairly steady and a bit of an older crowd. I guess there must be no schools nearby.

Noisiness - Not bad. About average for a steady Starbucks. The drive thru keeps them busy evidently. The music is definitely set to background level. I like that.

Ambience - Typical for Starbucks with modern, coffee-shop decor. 

Power - Power is available to pretty much all the seats, except the center ones. Wouldn't have far to stretch your power cord though.

Free wifi - Available and works well. No connection problems. A touch slow though.

Staff - It's adequately staffed with the usual, friendly staff you expect at Starbucks.

Seating - There are 17 seats inside and a patio with about 10 seats. There are two comfortable armchairs at a table and the square cafe tables are big enough you could probably fit two laptops with mice. Five of the chairs are at a bar, and I've never been a fan of them. In all the Starbucks locations I've been to, I'm not sure I've ever seen anyone actually use them.

Security - Seems fine. Not a bad area of the city, but you know the drill.

text:
Many of us coffee connoisseurs like to adventure out in our local city, state, or even the entire country for new and delicious coffee. But good ol Starbucks brings it back around every time with our usual orders and convenience. 

This location is in prime location to Park 100 and the highway. It offers a full circular parking lot around the entire building for ease of entering and leaving. This location I've found to be always clean and tidy. The amount of tables is larger than other local locations for those with work meetings, brunch, and just those who want to use free wifi. :) It is not the biggest Starbucks I have ever been to but I guarantee the capacity is above average.

text:
This location was formerly a Long John Silver's seafood fast food joint, believe it, or not. The building should have been leveled----It's not too late---and a nice, modern, INVITING space take it's place. There's currently no inside dining, which is fine by me as it doesn't give me a warm, cozy, feeling of cleanliness. So, no stars for the building.

I ordered a Venti iced coffee with ONE splash of pumpkin syrup. The splash is extra.

The associate was friendly and the staff was efficiently handling the drive thru which was wrapped around the small building (typical for this busy corner location).

I don't typically drink Starbucks; I'm not one to pay over $4 for a custom cup of iced coffee. Having enjoyed iced coffee from another well-known competitor recently, I happened to notice their Medium-sized iced coffee is about the same size as a Starbucks Venti, at half the cost. But, Starbucks coffee IS robust and really packs that caffeine punch.

text:
It's very hit or miss at this location, both with timeliness in being served as well as the employees making your morning beverage. For the most part, the better crew does tend to be there at least 3 out of 4 visits, so that's a plus.

I will second what Christopher said about the line though; it is super confusing for people not familiar with the area and I constantly see people asking "Is this the line? Or do I go back here?" What's the point in having a side door that opens half-way through the line that someone has to walk all the way back to the regular door to enter line. Why not just keep that door locked and cause less headaches? I've seen many a newcomer come in that way and look as dazed as a baby bird while they catch their barings and realize the actual line zips way back to the other door or catch them standing in the drink line when they have yet to place an order.

Signs or no, I've seen many people have issues with this; just saying, unless headache is one of many things you plan to pick up from starbucks during your morning routine, just enter through the regular door (across from Blue Mercury!)

text:
This is my favorite Starbucks in St. Louis.  I'm reviewing it as a Starbucks, since that is the sign on the side of the building.  I didn't expect to be blown away by some baristas creative take on a latte... but I also didn't expect to be underwhelmed by some hipster's weird interpretation of my favorite beverage. 

I go to Starbucks for consistency, friendly service and free refills.  I come here when I'm visiting my parents to get work done.  The staff has always been super friendly.  The decor in the Starbucks is nicer than some of the other stores I've been in.  This store is also a little less busy than their Kirkwood Rd. counterpart, so be sure to head here if you're going at a busy time of the day.  I think the drive thru helps manage the crowd.  

Overall, a great Starbucks.  You'll get the drink you want, and you'll get it with a smile from friendly staff.

text:
I drink Starbucks almost daily (it's my favorite coffee) and I stop at this location a couple times a month. While the staff is always friendly, the wait time is consistently and often considerably much longer then it should be and much longer than the Starbucks locations I stop at in Wilmington or any DD I stop at.

text: I splurged and went for a peppermint hot chocolate. It was a coldy morning and I was not in the mood for the coffee flavor. Either way it was tasty!

WIFI is uber reliable. I have never had trouble on that front.

text:
Starbucks stores are slowly getting the attitude that they're invincible. Like "you need us." And no apologies. Yes, there are exceptions. But I can lo grade more stores than hi these days. 
It's pretty foolish, as corporate empires take decades to build, and weeks to crumble, it seems. 
I think of what some knowledgeable person told me in Seattle. "We like to think of SBUX as the McDonald's of coffee - mediocre product available everywhere."
So to me, SBUX boils down to ambience and friendly service. And while they can precisely engineer the ambience of each store, the customer service is always the wild card in every case. 
This store, like so many others, is sadly one of the majority. Inconsistent cashier service, long times to make drinks, and indifference toward the sale are hallmarks.
Yes you have a few nice people behind the bar. But for the majority back there, it's the tired and true SBUX mantra: You Need Us.

text:
Look, we all know what Starbucks is like. This ain't no franchise, ladies and gentlemen, and there's nothing I can tell you about the selection that you don't already know.

What I CAN tell you is that this Starbucks is my absolute favorite nearby (half block away) establishment, with total credit due to the absurdly awesome manager and staff. Who else can I expect to harass me for being a bum freelancer, wearing the same baggy cutoff jorts every summer morning, and the same ripped jeggings every winter day? I mean BESIDES my mom. 

No one, that's who. 

I wouldn't have it any other way. These guys are friendly and fabulous, and make the morning drudge that much better.*

I happen to make an amazing cup o' joe -- beans purchased from a "source" who gets them  from Rulli of Marin County, California... fresh grinds every few days... single cup cone drip -- and on most mornings, I am happy to make my own. But at least once a week, when I've woken up on the wrong side of the bed and need a friendly face, or I've slept in and can't find the kettle (let alone those little cone filters) Wydown Starbucks comes through. 

-----

* To be fair, when you're a freelancer in cutoff jorts, there's not much "drudge" to the morning routine.

text:
Quick Tip: if you're a Starbucks connoisseur or have any desire for consistency in drink quality drive to Marsh Rd Starbucks!  

I had a really disappointing service experience at this new Starbucks location on a Saturday around 11:30 . I was excited that Delaware had more options for Starbucks now! ( Marsh rd and 896 95 rest stop are only real alternatives) The store location looked great inside and out. I was informed later, however, that the money put into the appearance and not into hiring REAL baristas is because this is not a REAL Starbucks, privately owned and operated.


 As soon as I received my drink I asked the Barista if it was foam free (as originally requested) she said it was. But when I picked it up the weight of the drink made it completely apparent that it was full of foam. I eventually posted the picture on twitter (below). When I asked the Barista to take out the foam I was shocked at her response! Wordless she grabbed the drink while I held the lid. She attempted to pour out the foam (which for obvious reasons did not work). She then scooped out the foam... then handed the half empty drink back to me. I asked that she just remake the drink. She once again surprised me, she just walked away! I couldn't believe it. Baristas have never been anything but kind and courteous even on their busiest days. I was the only one in the store, except for patrons sitting in the lounge (so it's not like I was monopolizing her time). 

The manager on duty then came over to make my drink from scratch a few seconds later. Guys, I can't make this up. When she handed me the drink it was so full of foam I begrudgingly asked AGAIN to have the foam removed, but this time I was explicit. I asked the foam please be scooped out and more soy milk steamed to be poured to the top. She smiled and started the drink. But then as I feared, these Baristas truly did not know how to serve a "no foam soy latte". The manager's attempt was half full and still had foam somehow. (Picture included ) This is when I decided to post the pictures to twitter. 

Then the manager proceeded to tell me I was wrong, when she saw me taking pictures, soy lattes "always produce foam" the manager told me. I drove 8 miles out of the way to go to a tried and true Starbucks where they made my drink. To add insult to injury the Baristas at the Riverfront Wilmington location didn't even offer me a refund WHEN I DIDN'T TAKE EITHER DRINK HOME. 

This was so unbelievably uncharacteristic of Starbucks I was shaken by the first Baristas aggressive nature and refusal to help with my drink and the manager's lack of ability to correct the situation. I really appreciate the Starbucks Twitter team reaching out right away after seeing the pictures. But ultimately Starbucks corp doesn't own this Starbucks and can't correct their obvious shortcomings.

text:
If I could give them less than one star, you bet I would. I've never had anything but problems at this Starbucks. While I've only been there twice during travel, I believe it is the worst run Starbucks I've ever been to. 
The first time: no cars in the drive thru, almost no one in cafe, yet we still got sandwiches that were burnt and bitter shots in our espresso drinks. I mean, if they're that slow, they can probably check my Bacon Gouda sandwich to make sure it even has bacon...Why is quality so crappy when the wait is nonexistent? 

The second time: just stopped by and had a huge line for a wait. Now, I understand that, it was a busy time and that's out of their control... but we ordered with no difficulty (and no screen to double check our order) and assumed everything would turn out okay... I was wrong. We ordered a triple venti cappuccino and instead got a triple venti americano which is MUCH different. You guys can at least double check the order at the box or at the window to assure the quality of your product, it's like your job or something.

I spent a few hours at the Starbucks the other day doing some work on the computer (sometimes you just have a different scenery) I noticed that this Starbucks is a neighborhood Starbucks pretty much the people that came in  know the employees. Me personally I'm not a huge Starbucks fan I don't drink a lot of coffee but the atmosphere is so calming and soothing and the smell of the coffee in the teas and pastries are somewhat relaxing. So what The Fall season they have all these new and  different coffee mixes for The Fall season that customers  are flocking in to get. 
 I decided to try the iced caramel macchiato with pumpkin sauce instead of vanilla let me tell you it was an acquired taste very different yet delicious.
 You can earn Starbucks rewards and have a Starbucks account and you can purchase really cool glasses and cups. They have an online store where you can purchase items and coffees of all different flavors.
 The holidays are coming soon so there will be all kinds of  gifts to purchase friends and family.
Although I'm not a coffee drinker I do love the Starbucks atmosphere.

text: I'm not a coffee drinker.  Imagine my confusion when I was told to buy a traveler jug of coffee and snacks for 4 people at 7 AM! They did a great job getting me out the door on time with a well organized jug.  You have my business next time I visit Tucson.

text:
Food: 4 Stars
Service: 3 Stars

I love their chai tea lattes, vanilla fraps, all of their seasonal drinks, and let's not forget their scrumptious snacks and sandwiches. The one thing I really DON'T love... the wait time. 

This location is not far from my office, so it's a perfect spot to grab a drink and go. Except that it's never a "grab and go" situation. 90% of the time it takes nearly 10 minutes to get my drink. (In a DRIVE-THRU!) Walking in and ordering over the counter is a tad better. While I understand that each drink is made to order and depending on what you order, it may take awhile for it to be made (several steps), it shouldn't take 10 minutes to get a drink. (I think the longest I've ever waited was 17mins in the drive-tru.)

Hardly convenient... :[

text:
Walking up to Starbucks at 3 AM some weeks ago, from a full table outside, I heard,

"VAGINA!"
"PUSSY!"
"PUSSY FOOT!"
"CHLAMYDIA!"

...WTF?  A minute later, I realized that they were playing a game like Taboo or Catch Phrase.  Apparently, they're ALWAYS there.  Good times.  Though I still wonder what that word was...

Yes, I'm writing yet another 5-star review for a Starbucks.  I mean, it's hard to give a place that's open 24/7 any other rating.  Plus, they've got cute, attentive baristas ready to serve you a tasty drink any hour of the day!  You can't beat that.  Definitely my Starbucks of choice when I'm in Tampa!

Sidenote: If you're going to sit outside, use bug spray.  In the scant few minutes that I sat outside to people-watch, I got four mosquito bites on my leg!

How is this Starbucks not already on Yelp??? This is my favorite Starbucks, hands down, in the city. I know, I know, all Starbucks are theoretically the same, so what makes this one special? Well, for one, the location. Prime location for those of us who live in my neighborhood. Also, the staff really make this place shine. As nearly every tip on Foursquare will tell you, Denise is the friendliest, most helpful barista in the city. She knows me by name and always rings up my usual order without me having to say a word. It's little touches like that that make this place great.

Aside from that, and the recent remodel that seemingly doubled the seating capacity, this place is your typical Starbucks, with all the usual food and beverage offerings. It's just a lot better than the one on Broad (no seating) or Chestnut (always completely dead, meh baristas).

text:
Lately I've been making it a habit to visit every Starbucks within the vicinity of places I visit. We were in Safety Harbor so I thought a quick Caramel Macchiato might be a great addition to my afternoon stroll. 

We walked in to a busy store that seemed seriously cramped. Although it has plenty of seating at either end, the line right in front of the counter is almost right against the parallel wall. Confused? Well let me explain what I mean by that.

If you come in from the front entrance, you have to walk past multiple tables and do a u-turn into the waiting line. That seems fine and dandy... unless there are people actually sitting at those tables. There is not enough walking room, and we were also toting our son in his car seat which lead to an embarrassing string of "Pardon me", "Excuse me", and "I'm sorry" as we tried to make our way through.

When I finally got to the counter I asked the barista about an offer I'd received via email. Before she even let me explain she cut me off and assumed that I'd meant the holiday drink bogo deal. I had to get out my phone and actually show the email to her before she knew what I was talking or even believed me. After that she seemed very huffy. When I asked her "So that means I can get any drink that has espresso in it, right?", she responded with, "Yeah, it's any of those as long as you give me the code". It wasn't her words that upset me; it was the way that she said them, as if I was annoying her. I was in no way rude or entitled in the way that I inquired about the email, so the attitude was not appreciated.

After I handed her the card and she slid it through the reader, she proceeded to tell me the price(after the discount) and went completely silent like she was waiting for me to pay. "Ma'am, I just let you swipe my Starbucks card. It has money on it", I said. Being as frustrated as I was, I really just wanted to get the heck out of there.

(Just a note: I came to find out later on in my Starbucks travels that the barista has to slide your card once for the discount and a second time for the payment. I was new to the Starbucks scene and another MUCH friendlier barista at another location explained the process to me at a later date.)

At least the drink turned out right. That's the only reason I gave it 2 stars instead of one. I'll come give this place another try sometime and hopefully I'll have a better experience. Can't let one bad apple spoil the bunch.

text:
Every since I found out about this new location, I have been coming here rather than the location on Grant & Swan. This location is A LOT more spacious and has more setting, compared to the other location. It also doesn't get as much foot traffic nor are there people just taking up space in the cafe just to use the free wi-fi.

It seems to be a lot cleaner and more efficient flow, since the counter space for the drink orders is bigger. Overall, there's not too much to stay about a Starbucks cafe, since they are all kind of modeled to fit a certain mold, but I do like this location more than the other one that is close by.

In terms of tables, they have a large central table with outlets underneath, and some other outlets set up around the cafe in the back and on the side. There's also a small stylish seating area in the corner with sofa/couch-like seats, for a more relaxed look and feel.

text:
Tuesday Afternoon Farmer's Market, perfect SB weather for all the cuckoo crays to come up out of the woodwork and invade State Street, and it seemed they were all in line at this particular Starbucks to use the two unisex restrooms who's numerical locking passcodes were taking the day off. 
I knew this experience had an unforeseen idiotic outcome as soon as I walked into the door behind Mr. and Mrs. Bewildered Halfwits discombobulated by either Starbucks in general or just being around people cuz they lacked any clue as to what the next step was once they got in the door. Barista finally succeeds in focusing their attention on his task at hand ( which is finding out why they're in his breathing space, taking their money, and moving right along to Moi, who knows what she's ordering) FINALLY! My turn at the counter, yay me! Until I hear the words I didn't want to hear "Sorry ma'm we are out of Very Berry Hibiscus Refresher" Boo!!! 
Meanwhile, the place is swamped full of disrespectful marionettes of all walks of life and age groups, and they're all waiting to use above mentioned restrooms. An eternity passes, and it's my turn. I'm in there about 90 seconds give or take, and someone's banging on the door. Uh hello Miss Impetuous, if you took five seconds to LOOK before you start your spazz on the door, you may see the conspicuously bold "IN USE" I walked out, slammed the door so it would lock, (oops, my bad! Now you've gotta go to the counter and pray you'll get the attention of busy baristas for an unlock, I would've made sure to pass the door to you. But why should I? Your ignorance landed you here sweetie) and walked out of there before the scenery would start affecting my brainwaves. With all the sbucks all over State Street, I will choose wisely next time and keep this one in my rearview.

This Starbucks is never super crowded (and I typically go in the morning-prime coffee hour).  The service is always fast and the staff is always friendly. text:
I tend not to frequent this Starbucks do to the horrendous parking, but I was in the mood for some Starbucks so I decided to stop off at this location. 

First, as I mentioned before the parking here is bad. There are maybe 5-10 spots at the most and it results in chaos. I ended up parking across the street due to joe filled up the lot was. 

While the parking here is horrendous, the staff and food are up to par. 

I got an everything croissant, bacon Gouda breakfast sandwich and a strawberry smoothie. They also had a large variety of other baked goods in their case. 

All my food came out fast and was very tasty. I loved the everything croissant. It was soft and had a variety of delicious toppings including sesame seeds, poppy seeds and some garlic. The smoothie was solid and on par with other Starbucks smoothies that I have had.

The staff here is very good as they are polite and professional. They also serve the food and drinks very fast. Mike was great! 

This Starbucks also has a small courtyard with some tables in the back. 

Altogether, this Starbucks has terrible parking, but the rest of the restaurant is fine.

text:
Since the addition on the space next door, this Starbucks is the largest I've been to outside of NYC. It's so spacious, bright and clean inside. I love the 2 long high communal tables and tons of electric outlets placed under them. The only complaint I have, which is the same for all Starbucks, is the temperature. It's FREEZING in there. It might actually be colder at this location than most. Recently I was there for 2 hrs, even with a cardigan on, I left a popsicle. I'm not kidding.

Why 4 stars and not 5? A couple of employees could be more cheerful and sociable. They actually look like they don't enjoy working, period. The whole time I was never, I never saw either of them smiled once. Maybe I shouldn't be too critical as long as my drinks are done right.

The parking lot is still small since this Starbucks is in a tiny plaza. There's no changing this since there's no room to expand around this plaza. I usually park in the back in case people scratch or hit my car when they try to squeeze into the small spaces.

Looking forward to hanging at this location more. And I'll be bringing a coat. Ha!
