text:
It just like any other Starbucks really so not much to comment there. However, I came here on a Saturday or Sunday (can't remember) and the line to order took 15 minutes. No joke, 15 minutes. By the time I got to the cash register to order, I ask her how long it would take to get a drink and she said about 20/30 minutes. I have no problem waiting for a good meal etc but waiting almost 40 minutes for coffee is absurd.

Not sure what they need to do to rectify this so it doesn't happen again (hire more people? expand the space?) I don't know, but waiting that long for a drink is quite ridiculous

Have you ever gotten so busy and distracted through the course of your day that you forgot to drink any coffee, and then started to completely run out of gas in the late afternoon?  That happened to me today while I was shopping, and I managed to trek the fifty yards or so from Eddie Bauer to here without calling an ambulance ;-)

I give Starbucks points for having locations pretty much everywhere on the Planet, and this one turned out to be convenient in a pinch, but the coffee here (and at all the other links in the chain) is just AOK at best, and considering the prices, the value proposition is questionable at best.

Bottom line: If you need a decent cup of Joe, you can do worse than Starbucks, but you can do a heck of a lot better as well.

text: I went to this Starbucks location last night for just a cookie or any type of dessert/pastry.  I was craving something sweet, so pretty much anything would've done.  I walked inside at about 7:30 PM and step in front of the refrigerated case to find it empty.  I stared at it for the longest time wondering if this location closed at 8 PM maybe and I'd just missed them putting away all the food for the night or if maybe they'd had a whole tour bus stop by that wiped them out or something.  It was probably 5 minutes that I stood there.  Not one employee ever addressed me or my confused expression at the empty case, and I eventually ended up just leaving and getting a delicious, fresh chocolate chip cookie from the Puccino's down the street. text:
Starbucks, due to its success, sets the bar against all other coffee shops are measured by. But how does Starbucks compare to Starbucks? Well, THIS Starbucks compares very well.

As far as food and beverage quality goes, Starbucks is remarkably consistent store to store. So, if you like the Starbucks taste you will like it here. If you don't then you won't like it here either.

As far as location and interior décor this Starbucks is as good as any other. The parking lot is a little awkward, but for the most part this Starbucks delivers pretty much exactly what you expect.

The service here, however, is exceptional - even when compared to other Starbucks. My server (Shae, I believe) wasn't merely pleasant, but genuinely seemed happy to see me. She took obvious pride in her mission to serve others and add just a little bit of extra comfort and happiness in the world. In today's self-entitled world, precious few understand the true dignity that serving others affords.

I walked into this Starbucks with high expectations of their quality of product.  Those expectations were exceeded by their quality of heart.

text:
I am a daily weekday customer of this Starbucks location, so if this review were anything OTHER than five stars you'd better believe I'd be a walking contradiction. 

The success of this particular Starbucks may seem to be due to its centralized South Tampa location, but that is only partially true.  The rest of its success can be attributed to its excellent management and staff. 

The manager Ian and all of his baristas make a solid effort to get to know  you - and not just how to spell your name (an effort that I have come to appreciate given the unique spelling of mine), but they also make a considerable effort to get to know who you are as a person. I would love to report that this is just my experience, but I have personally overheard more than one barista ask various customers, "Hey (insert any name you can think of), the usual?" Sometimes, they're already preparing the drink as the person walks in. This type of familiarity is hardly achievable at most modern-day bars, let alone a "chain" coffee shop! 

On a recent occasion, a newer barista forgot my breakfast sandwich order. Upon realizing her mistake, she offered me a $5 gift card for my wait and trouble (which I graciously accepted...my daily visits make for an expensive habit!) The aforementioned is only one of many examples that support my assertion of a five star experience. 

One last thing - re: the bathroom code; I am honestly shocked that it took Starbucks so long to implement a secured bathroom system. While I lived in NYC, Starbucks was referred to as "the public restroom of the city." This is no joke and it is no surprise - heck, there was one on nearly every corner!  If you are actually a CUSTOMER and not just popping by in a drunken stupor post-Gasparilla or after your generic walk/run on Bayshore, then asking for the code shouldn't be that hard now, should it?

text:
A decent spot to grab a cup of coffee or some food before going through the day. This Starbucks is part of the University of the Sciences and will host a plethora of students. It will get crowded and loud during the rush, but slow down as people go to class. Staff are usually friendly depending on the day and service is quick   

For students:  
This starbucks is similar to regular ones, with the perks of being full of your classmates. You can use your declining balance or Devils dollars here. A great spot to get quick tasty breakfast. Literally the best option for food in general on this campus that doesn't cost you real money. 

For non students:
This location is nothing compared to a non university affiliated starbucks, but  you can always get the same options you can at other locations. They will take cash and card and treat you no different. There are usually many students here who will either be studying or eating, so seating can be tricky. Deffinitely a go to when you visit the school. 

Phone wi-fi does not work here for some reason. There just doesn't seem to be service and nothing ever connects. Laptops do work here however. 

This is a pretty nice locstion and typical location for a University. Better food here than most places and a nice atmosphere.

text:
When did Starbucks become so slow? 

This has become my local Starbucks and I am
In here almost once a week lately for meetings. It's large, modern and spacious inside, with lots of work areas to choose from, but it's constantly busy, so chances of snagging one depends on when you arrive. 

With the volume they seem to have at all times of the day, they never seem to be adequately staffed to handle it. The line is 5-10 customers deep at the register inside and my experience is they only have one register running. The staff is always buzzing about behind the counter preparing drinks that are all seemingly for the drive through and keeping the operation going. If you're sticking around though, there's always a long wait for drinks. 

Today my drink was all sorts of wrong too, which hasn't happened before. I ordered a nitro cold brew and it arrived as an iced cold brew. You can't put ice in a nitro drink because it takes all the nitro goodness out. 

All in all this Starbucks just doesn't have it going on. I've become used to it though and accepted this as the standard at this location.

text:
Stargetbucks...Lol, this is our nickname for this Starbucks which is located inside Target on Sierra Center Parkway. Great location and what better way to get a little pick me up when heading in to do a little shopping! Staff is usually very friendly and quick to get their orders out but can you please get rid of that large awkward order monitor that you have to look around to see me when I tell you my order? On a more personal note, I've been to a lot of Starbucks locations but this is the first Starbucks I have been to that does not ask for your name... I asked "don't you need my name..." and the cashiers response was "nope, your good" When there a lot of people standing around waiting for their drinks it would be nice to know who owns that order. 

If the order is called out for a certain drink and someone else ordered the same size and drink as you... there is a moment of awkwardness when you both head up to get 'that' drink they called out...personal experience. text:
Very friendly baristas here, I'm usually greeted when I arrive. 
Plenty of free parking.
Plenty of indoor working area space and seating space. 

The drink quality on Saturday afternoons however is unpredictable. 
I've even had one barista who was reluctant to remake a drink. I order no foam lattes (I don't get how people like drinking a drink that contains 1/4 foam) and you always know when there's foam... Not only is the drink significantly lighter but you can see foam when you lift the lid! When I asked the barista to remake it she insisted on explaining why there was foam instead of saying "yes". Of course I understand why foam is produced but that has nothing to do with fulfilling a customer's "no foam" request. Steam more milk, use a spoon to remove any foam, fill with milk... 

Needless to say the drink was remade without foam but not without some coaxing :-/

Writing this review from inside the store. Spent a LONG time waiting in line. The cashier Ricky didn't understand our order 2x, a supervisor came over to help him enter Stevia for our drink. That's not an uncommon request, I think he was in training. With the line nearly extended to the front door, probably safe to say they were understaffed and perhaps a slower cashier should not be used during busy time.

Come to my order. According to the same employee that helped cashier enter Stevia into order system, she said their blender was broken and they could not make foam for the pumpkin cream cold brew. Seemed very unapologetic and just said I could get a refund. Refund given, waited for my mom's portion of the order; I left empty handed because of their short commings.

A refund, apology, and a "would you like something else for the inconvenience" would have gone a long way considering how dragged out this road trip detour was. 

Avoid this location.

text: Nothing better on a hot day than your fave beverage - unless the staff gets it wrong and you realize that they don't know their products.  I ordered a Venti iced Earl Grey tea (I repeated this a few times as the screen said "BlkTea") with heavy cream and sugar free vanilla syrup.  I know it takes a few minutes for the Earl Grey to steep, so when it was handed to me nearly immediately, I asked when they started keeping iced Earl Grey on hand.  The girl working the window said "Uh, I don't know...?" and the barista who had made the drink said "we always have our iced black tea - it's an English Breakfast blend".  I responded that I didn't order black tea, I ordered Earl Grey, and the girl with the headset said "I thought Earl Grey WAS black tea". I responded that though they're both made with black tea, Earl Grey has bergamot and other flavors and is a different tea.  Instead of offering to remake my drink correctly, and since I had already paid, she just smiled and said "sorry, I thought it was the same thing." I had a guest in my car and a line behind me so I left, but I am disappointed that the staff don't know their beverages and I was not offered the correct beverage OR a refund - I was just smiled at like "have a nice day with your wrong drink!"

text:
I probably won't make it a habit of reviewing restaurant or coffee chains on Yelp, but I wanted to make an exception for this particular Starbucks because the employees here are really fantastic. Every time I visit, no matter how groggy, incoherent, or rushed I am--the baristas are always friendly and their pleasant demeanor is like an extra pick-me-up on top of the caffeinated deliciousness that I purchased that day. 

The new remodeling is great too! It seems to be a much better usage of space! Speaking of space, I've read a lot of reviews that knock down stars due to the parking situation that they can't really do anything about. They are on a busy street on the edge of a neighborhood, that's just the nature of the area I feel. If you want more of a parking challenge, try for the one downtown! Or if you want easier parking, try the one further north on the same street. Its Starbucks, if you've been driving in circles because the lot's full, you can drive 5 minutes to the next one. Just PLEASE don't be one of those people that sit in their car and decide to make a phone call, do their taxes, clip their nails, or whatever after picking up a coffee. Be courteous of other people trying to park and move out! /end rant. ;)

So, the explanation for not giving it full 5 stars, I really think they should put some sort a railing or one of those metal dividing "fences" along their patio area like you see at other Starbucks. Sometimes one can feel a bit insecure sitting so close to traffic, so for this reason I try to avoid sitting outside at this location. :/

text:
Kudos to the baristas on my last visit. Still squinting against the cosmic rays of COVID-19's corona, they've opened the doors, but only to the extent of the long arm of the law: one giant step from the entryway to the interior's antisocial barrier. Waiting for service at a nearby car dealership whose coffee maker was removed against fear of the virus sent me over the hill through invisible woods to grab a cuppa. The Buck's brew is more to my liking, anyway.

The barista's voice smiled from beneath the facemask as she retrieved my mobile order. The drive-through lane was (surprisingly) empty and no one allowed inside, so service was immediate. The green to-go stopper (so often out-of-stock in busier pasts) had been pre-inserted by a magical, plasticized hand. Condiments are also banned to public fingers, so you'll need to ask specifically for anything you'd normally manage on your own at the walkup counter. Yet, a cheery and successful visit was had by all.

Your mileage will vary. Like its sibling locations, service varies with each crew and visit. It's still a Starbucks cookie-cutter, chain store experience. But we must all do our part to keep the beans brewing. Mischief managed.

text:
This is easily my favorite Starbucks in UC and possibly in Philly overall. Used to sit here all day during undergrad to meet up with friends and get hours of reading/studying done. This was always my secret spot during finals, and I even make it a point to visit whenever I'm back in Philly :)

What makes it different - it's clean and a good size (not too crowded) and the staff is friendly. I also love the location; good midpoint between Penn and Drexel and close to Wawa, Sangkee, and other food places if you're looking to take a break while studying. There's another Starbucks a block away on Walnut but it's usually more crowded (although the Walnut Starbucks is more convenient for grabbing drinks in between classes). What I like most about this Starbucks is that it has plenty of natural sunlight and gives off more of a small coffeehouse vibe compared to other [more generic] locations.

A couple of considerations - It gets cold during the winter due to all the windows and the lights are pretty dim in the evening.

text:
This is a great Starbucks.  The employees are good, and friendly, too.  The inside has the modern but comfortable look and feel of new Starbucks locations.  And they keep the place (including the bathrooms) clean. Most importantly (for the purposes of my Starbucks rankings), they have a number of electrical outlets, including a pair of outlets (plus a pair of USB charging ports) in each of the four legs of the communal table.

This location has a respectable food selection and the surprisingly good nitro brew.  They have consistently gotten my order right.  It's everything that I look for in a Starbucks.

FTR, they also have a drive-through.  I don't do drive-through anything, so I can't rate that, but they seem to manage it decently since it never spills over to cause problems for the customers inside.

New Orleans is not the kind of place to go to Starbucks (at least not in the French Quarter).  This Starbucks (inside The Shops at Canal Place) is testament to why. text: Great staff for the most part, though there is a heavier set female (manager?) who seems to be regularly less than approachable.  The service is fast and the quality of drinks is up to par with all the other Starbucks in town.  The shortcomings, I believe, are more about the commercial space than any one person's failing.  Long, thin layout with the milk, cream, etc. placed far away from the attendants and by the front door makes for a difficult time fixing up your coffee.  Plus when a container needs to be refilled, or a spill needs to be cleaned, the staff is sort of far away and harder to grab for attention.  Lastly, and unfortunately, is an element they have no control over.  There is a large homeless encampment in the nearby neighborhood, so there is a much more colorful and sometimes uncomfortable atmosphere in the cramped quarters there.  I still go there regularly, however.  No glaring reason not to, just not the great pearl of local Starbucks locations.

text:
A rest stop Starbucks is not my favorite option, however, when you have been on the road awhile a hot cup of coffee sounds like heaven.

Because this Starbucks is at a rest stop the lines and crowds can be a bit much.  You will also encounter people that are not used to Starbucks which add to wait times and your frustration level as the barista explains the menu to each person in front of you.

text: You think you've seen one Starbucks, you've seen 'em all, right?   Wrong.   This is absolutely the fastest coffee shop I have ever been to.   On a busy Wednesday morning, there were 2 cashiers and 2 (maybe 3) baristas moving at warp speed.   I ordered a coffee and a breakfast sandwich, and before I even put my wallet back in my pocket, the cashier was handing me my coffee.   Then I walked around to the sugar/cream station and as soon as I had put the lid back on top of my coffee, I heard "slow-roasted ham & swiss!" and was out the door.   Total time inside: less than 2 minutes.   Amazing.

I like all the entertaining reviews for this place, though I can't say I have anything to add to them. 

We just came here for a grand latte and my usual "iced-venti-half-caf-Americano-with-three-pumps-sugar-free-Vanilla-with-light-ice-and-light-room" and left unscathed. This place was clean when we were there (a weekend) and our orders were quickly prepared and as-ordered.

I do wonder if Yelpers are being a little hard on this guy Alberto. Even though I have never met him, I feel bad for him. No one knows his story, what goes on in his life, or what he might be dealing with. 

Nonetheless, judging from the gross disapproval of his patrons, I hope he is able to improve his performance or find a role that is suited to his occupational strengths.

text:
I do hope a manager reads this.

Someone had scheduled two sweet young, very young, adults to run this fairly large location on a Friday afternoon. Well, they can't run a Starbucks without some adult and experienced management and supervision!

Here is the detail of our experience. We got there around 4:00 p.m., ordered two coffees, as the barista was pouring half way - she realized she was out of coffee, and the baristas looked at each other, and said oh, sorry forgot to brew new coffee. 

We waited, normally as a courtesy, they could have given us a credit, but hey it's only coffee. Fine.  
When the coffee brewed, she added the new coffee it IN the old half-full cup. I said, but that was stale, and cold... she was surprised. The baristas were nice, but were soooo out of it. I am not sure how they were running the place. We were there a good 10- 15 minutes... Every transaction could have been a story told.

Ok, we got our coffees, and left. Then I decided to go back and get the store number, as each Starbucks store has a location number, I had to contact their manager. They didn't know their store number.

Ugghhhh.... I know it's Starbucks, sometimes it is hit and miss. But really? You leave a store so neglected, serve expired cold coffee, and be oblivious to why we are disappointed?

text:
Stupid me..   Why did I go to this location...  It's always given me problems...  Even the wife said, "I thought you were going to the other one..."  Our breakfast plans fell through, we needed coffee and we were headed home.  We decided to use up a starbucks gift card and we ordered coffee and sandwiches.  Impressively, the sandwiches were done in seconds and we had them before we even paid.  Then we waited for over seven minutes and the sandwiches were cold by the time we ate them...   We got our drinks and went home to eat.  While drinking our drinks in the car, we noticed both of our drinks were wrong.  The cashier wrote JCF, for Java Chip Frappe on our drinks, but the person making them thought the J was a V I think...  Both of our drinks lacked Java, but were completely vanilla beaned up...  

So why didn't we go back to fix the error?  Well, this location and parking lot is the absolute worst.  Once we were driving down Germantown, we would need to make an illegal u-turn, then go to the mall to turn around, just to get back here.  With the traffic already building, it would add twenty minutes to our trip.  Forget it...  

As a side note, I did complain online to Starbucks and they gave me $15 back.  We spent slightly over $20, but I'm satisfied with that resolution.  We won't spend that $15 at this location.  Karma once again for not listening to myself or you other yelpers...

This is probably my favorite Starbucks.  I travel quite frequently and make it a habit to stop here on my way to baggage claim since I have time to kill; the St Louis airport takes 30-40 minutes for your bags to come out.  That's another story though.

Anyway, what I like about this Starbucks is how friendly the staff is.  There's always the same two girls working here and they are always happy and cheerful.  And, they provide great service. He was very apologetic about the fact that they only had the sugar free syrup, and I was kind of apprehensive about that but it actually still worked. text:
It's difficult to be fair when discussing Starbucks!

The name is so polorizing.  True coffee lovers, people who really know and love coffee, who believe, correctly or not that Italy is the only country in the world where you can't get a bad cup.  Who only buy their beans at LeColombe or have them Fed-Xed from David Schermer in Seattle, who have a definite ratio in their heads of what % robusto and what % arabica makes the best brew. These people hate Starbucks.  They vilify their "baristas", they rail against their penchant for over roasting, their fascination for stupid names, their insane list of combinations and their have-a-nice-day-robotic-Stepford staff.  

The rest of us are thankful that Starbucks, regardless of how they did it, raised the bar on coffee excellence in this country.  No one can deny that before them, 98% of the coffee served in America was swill.  Now, as a direct result of the marketing juggernaut they have become, even WaWa serves coffee that is better than good restaurants served less than 15 years ago.  OK, they still have to work on getting rid of the flavors, but it's a valid point.

For those of us in Philly, we don't have to go to a Starbucks so long as we can get to, Anthony's on Ninth Street, Le Colombe on Rittenhouse Square and Manayunk, Cafe Fulya on Second Street, The Last Drop on Pine, Ants Pants on South, Philadelphia Java Company on Fourth and on Second, Fitzwater Cafe on Seventh & Fitz, Saxby's in Haverford and a few others that I know when I drive by, and certainly Ray's on Ninth for if there is cleaner coffee making apparatus in the city, I'd like to see it,  But in a pinch, with my daily schedule sometime denying me access to these quality coffee houses, I get a short one at the local SB fully understanding that the good houses got pinched into action by that audacious company out of Seattle.

Love them or hate them, we, the coffee drinkers have benefited by them.

text:
I've been visiting Starbucks recently because it's closest to my work. So when I need a quick fix, I know I can run to Starbucks and back. And I have to say: they've really improved their iced coffee blend. It's not nearly as bitter as it used to be, to where now I can drink it without adding milk. It used to taste much more like an iced Americano - dark and bitter - but now it's significantly lighter, with a smoother flavor to it. So I will continue to go back to Starbucks when need be.

Now, I do have one complaint - or more of a general warning. If you've never been to this location - or any of the other businesses in this strip - the parking can be horrendously competitive during peak hours. It can quickly devolve into chaos, where people park anywhere just to get a "spot." So if you are going to this location, keep that in mind. It's better to go during irregular times than face the battle that is that parking lot.

text:
I can easily see this as being one of my favorite Starbucks.

When I first got here, I was surprised that there is a drive-through -- I 've never seen that in a Starbucks before. In other coffee shops, yes, but not at a Starbucks. But then, the Starbucks I used to frequent were all in a very walkable, not very car-friendly city. There is a guy who stands outside of the place, within easy reach of the drive-through customers -- I don't know what he's selling or asking money for, but I've seen customers give him money.

I liked their outdoor seating. There are enough places for a few dozen people. It's shaded, but perhaps the sun will hit people at certain times of the day. In the center of the seating is a fireplace. I have yet to experience how cold the winters will get here, but I can see the draw of this (if they use it).

Inside is a typical Starbucks. On the left, it looks like an office lobby: Comfortable chairs for reading. On the right are the expected rows of tables and people all absorbed in their laptops. I'm assuming the place has air conditioning, if only because it didn't feel hot or stuffy inside.

The pastry offering is not extensive as some Starbucks. But it has the basic donuts and cheese danishes and cakes and muffins that I've seen elsewhere. I was only on a breakfast mission, so I did not see if there were any sandwiches on display.

The staff who took my order were very friendly. No problems with my order whatsoever.

If anything, I happily felt like a Luddite, as I whipped out my old-fashioned book and read it outside in gorgeous weather while the people inside were hunched over their laptops.

text: I'm not really sure what happened to this Starbucks in the three months since I have been here (I worked down the block and would frequent it a few times a week) but it went from being one of the better Starbucks I have visited to absolutely disgusting. I visited at about 7:30 on my way to work (they tend to get slammed right around 8:00 and you shouldn't even bother at that hour) and there were maybe three people in line. The first thing I noticed was the shelves were pretty bare and the sundries were barely stocked which has never been the case before. Ordered my food and the staff was friendly enough. Mostly just going through the motions. When I went to put milk in my coffee is when I realized just how dirty the store was. That are looked like it hadn't been cleaned nor the trash been taken out in at least two days. Most of the milk containers were empty. Nothing had been wiped down in a good minute. I proceeded to look around after that and I have never seen it this dirty before. To top it off the bathroom had no toilet paper and the back up roll was also empty. I don't know if it's bad management or lazy employees but it has suffered greatly in just a few short months. Had I written this review six months ago it would be nothing but praise.

text:
Indie hangout and gathering place for hundreds of delinquent teens on a Friday or Saturday. The time I went there I was on a bike ride through Bucks county and was clad in bright blue spandex racing kit. I was in queue waiting and the guy in front of me was paying for a few coffees with several methods of payment: IE starbucks card, cash and credit card-just for a $20.00 tab. I was patient and when my turn was up next, the barrista (coffee waitress) looked at me and went to the person in back of me and said: "your usual Fran?" It was like I was invisible..I doubt that, as I was wearing a bright blue uniform. I complained to the manager and didn't even get a free cup of coffee.

The barristas are pretentious and only seem to want to wait on regulars. I intentionally ask for a "small" or a "large" instead of using their terminology of "vente" and "grande." With the cadre of teens on a weekend night, I avoid the place.

text:
Pros: the staff here are super friendly and maintain their composure during very busy times.

Cons: it often takes 10-20 minutes to get a regular or iced coffee if you order at the counter. Also, some of the staff seem to have a knowledge gap when it comes to drinks and such.  One guy was unsure how many espresso shots were in a drink and another person didn't know which sugar free syrups you had and just said "I think it's only vanilla" he didn't bother to ask or check.  However, the most frustrating thing is the QA with some of the products.  The last few times I have been in there the iced coffee was practically see-through it was so light. It seems like they added too much water to it.  I've been drinking black iced coffee at Starbucks for about 20 years, at locations all over the country, so I'm not just being a Karen.  I've actually had to ask them to replace it with cold brew, which usually looks better.  

I want to give them 5 stars because most of the people are nice overall, but some of the service aspects are lacking.  Hope they fix these things.

text:
Unlike some of the other reviews for this location, I'm not going to slam them for how busy they are. Actually that is one of their pros in my book. Yes they have long lines. Some days I'd even guess that there are 50 people in line and 20 people that have already ordered and are waiting for their drinks. Considering how many people are in line, I think they do a good job keeping things moving.

What I am going to slam them for is their drink consistency issues. Just because the place is slammed does not mean corners should be cut and quality should decrease. 

After a few incorrectly made drinks, I quickly learned to order drinks without any changes (I'm not talking about which kind of milk). By changes I means adding an extra shot of caramel, or substituting one ingredient for another. Also, god forbid  you ask for a sugar in your drink because they won't do it. You'll just have to put it in yourself (which isn't the worst thing in the world).

The one thing they did that really annoyed me was ruin my hot tea. I didn't think anyone could ruin tea. You get hot water and put a bag in it. It normally takes about 2 seconds to make it. Which is why I knew something was wrong when it was taking much longer than usual. It turns out the the barista read the cup incorrectly and gave me a tea latte. Now, I didn't have time to make a fuss, but from that point on I watch them like a hawk and really emphasize the point that I just want HOT TEA, not a HOT TEA LATTE.

text:
I had a gift card to use and was in the mood for Starbucks so we came here. My husband said this was the most people he's seen in this line. It was moving really slow too. We get to the cashier and finally order. 

The cashier was either not interested in her job at all or just flaky. I had to repeat my order a couple times, she was arguing with another worker as I was giving my order and not listening. I said I wanted the bacon egg and cheese on the croissant and she seemed like she didn't know what I was talking about. I had to point to the display counter. When she decided to give me her attention again I said I also wanted the bacon and gruyere sous vide egg bites. She seemed confused again then said "oh...ok. Which one?" The egg bites were good and the sandwich was ok, but for the price we can probably make our own at home and not have to deal with incompetent workers. Next time we decide to go to Starbucks I wanna try to go to a different location, even if we have to drive farther.

text:
Starbucks Ratings: 3.8/5

Food: 3.75/5 - Decided to grab some coffee from Starbucks when we are waiting for brunch at Ruby's Slipper. I got the iced coffee with a pump of sugar free vanilla syrup and light ice. We were hungry, so I also got two bakery items to share with my siblings. Blueberry muffin is good and moist, a classic at Starbucks, but it was a bit too sweet for my liking; wished there weren't that much sugar crystals on top of it. The banana nut loaf was better, once again super moist with a strong banana flavor. We all love the crunch from the nuts on the loaf, and it was a solid size too. Pro tip, always ask them to warm up your bakery items. 

Ambiance: 4/5 - Located on Canal Street, probably one of the biggest Starbucks in the area. It is also a tourist area, so expect many people there. Interior is very spacious with lots of seats and room to work or chill, similar to most Starbucks locations but just larger. Inside is very festival, bright, and pretty clean. 

Price: 3.75/5 - Got two bakery items: blueberry muffin and banana nut muffin for $2.75 and $3.25 respectively, and a Grande iced coffee for $2.95. The total came up to be around $12. This Starbucks's bakery items and coffee are probably cheaper or around the same as other local coffee shops, but if you're visiting I'd recommend try out a local shop instead.  

Service: 3.5/5 - Staffs were okay, not greatest like most Starbucks locations. I ordered through the mobile app, and got called when my orders are ready, but there weren't as much acknowledgement from the staffs when we walked it, so not really amicable to the customers. 

BLUF: If you are staying on Canal Street in NOLA and want to grab a quick morning coffee, this is a very convenient location for you. Very spacious with seats against the windows and middle section. Essential pit stop for a dose of caffeine and small snack.

text:
You would think that this Starbucks would be awesome because they are usually busy, have great local and tourist customers, and there are several other locations in a 3 block radius. WRONG. This Starbucks is THE worst Starbucks I have EVER been to. I have worked on State Street for years and every time I give them a chance, they always let me down. I used to only drink Starbucks coffee, but this location has made me learn to love Coffee Bean, which is right across the street. 
Here are the worst parts of the service they provide (all of these things happen 90% of the time, no joke):
1. They act like taking your order is a nuisance. They roll their eyes and sigh if you take longer than 2 seconds to order.
2. They take FOREVER to make your order. I'm not talking 3, 5, or 7 minutes. They literally take 20 mins to make you a drink. It is because they don't care. I really think they hate making coffee here. i have seen them, with a super long line, have 2 people taking orders, 1 person doing nothing, and 1 person making all drinks. They do not care if you have somewhere to be, if you are on a break, if they are busy, or anything else about your life. They move only at 1 speed: DGAF.
3. The reason I write my review: Today, I went to get a coffee on my break. I was too lazy to walk the few extra feet to a different Starbucks or CBTL. I always regret when I do this. I gave my order and endured the normal attitude and eye rolling that is expected. I waited for the baristas to continue their personal conversations with their friends seated at the bar. When my 10 minute iced coffee was finally ready, the girl did not even break eye contact, let alone talking, set my coffee out and THREW my straw at me. THREW it. like tossed it so that it fell to the edge of the table. She didn't say my name. She didn't say my order. The signal to grab my drink wasn't a smile or eye contact. It was a thrown straw.
I left disgusted that I just paid $3.95 to be insulted!! I know that it's just coffee, but this day in age, when times are "tough", the one luxury we all have is to spend our money where we choose. When something is so prevalent and there are so many options, stores should not rest on the fact that people "need" what they have. Because the truth is, we don't. I gave this Starbucks 3 years and probably close to 100 visits to earn my business and today it was literally a straw that broke the camel's back. 
DON"T GO HERE.

text: Starbucks in the Cherry Hill mall is very hit or miss. They're either empty or the line is so long it extends out of the entrance to the Macy's it's located in. Due to the stand's small size, there are probably 3-4 employees working at the maximum. But even with 2 on the register and 2 behind the counter it's not enough to accommodate the business of this location. Also, the space where customers wait to order and wait to pick up their order is cramped. It's not uncommon for people to resort to standing outside of the Macy's and try to listen carefully if their name is called, rather than elbowing strangers in the ribcages just to wait for a frappuccino. The drink taste like any other Starbucks. The baked goods are usually fully stacked and the employees are very helpful and nice. I give them props for being able to serve their coffee with a smile given the factors that make this location inefficient. I believe the mall would benefit greatly if they simply opened up a large independent Starbucks store within the mall.

text:
I'm hesitant to write a review of this place as I'm afraid more people will come here to study lol. But in all seriousness, this is one of my favorite places to study. It's typically empty enough to get a decent seat and power outlet. The area is large and spacious so you don't feel claustrophobic. The inside is not as nice looking as Buddy Brew...it just feels/looks like a typical Starbucks. The other Starbucks on Westshore (next to Panera) has a cooler feel but it is way smaller. 

They have about 8 tables along a large couch which has outlets for each table. There are also some tables along the wall which have power outlets as well. The bathrooms (1 for women and 1 for men) have been clean the times I have been here. The staff is friendly and drinks (depending on how busy they are) are served relatively fast. I feel if you're inside the drinks come out slower compared to the drive thru. 

I can see parking may be an issue but the times I have come, parking was a breeze. Drive through can definitely be busy during peak times but which Starbucks isn't?

text: I stopped in at what was  the original Delaware County Starbucks.  It has been updated a few times since it first opened.  They stuck with only tables on the last two renovations.   I hadn't been here in a while but stopped in while waiting for a friend to finish their appointment a few doors down.   It was a Friday evening and I was a bit disappointed.    There were several people working the counter and I got good service and good coffee.   Why then was I unhappy, you are thinking?   As I am waiting, cruising the Internet, I am in the company of 6 or so pubescent tweeners who are talking drugs and private parts to the extent that in any other location, this would be sexual harassment.   Thank goodness it only lasted 15 minutes, but I was surprised that the staff just ignored it and let it go on.   After they left, I was inundated by two more sets of teenagers.  Not a place for adult drinkers like the Havertown location....

text:
I dropped into this Starbucks the other day at the request of a friend I was driving with. I don't usually stop into Starbucks, as I make my coffee at home. I decided rather than loiter while my friend got coffee that I'd grab a bag of their coffee and a breakfast sandwich to boot.

When I handed the coffee to the cashier he was kind enough to ask me if I wanted it ground. Oops, good question, I didn't even notice it wasn't ground, and I sure don't have a grinder at home. That was a point right there, but then I ate the sandwich and it was surprisingly good!

The biggest surprise was the coffee once I brewed it! I got the medium "house" blend I believe, and I think I'll be buying it again.

This review is based on the quality of the food and the coffee, the cleanliness of the joint (OK, it just opened), and the friendly, helpful staff. You just found a new customer (at age 42 no less).

text:
This is a Starbucks like many others.  The baristas change from time to time as most of them do not choose to serve coffee for their lifelong career.  I've never had an issue with the service here.  My issue is with the HORRIBLE PARKING.  The lot is TINY and cars are often backed up onto Rt 30 waiting for someone to make a 5 point turn.  Cars double park, hog the "15 minute" spots, and basically turn this corner into a nightmare.  
There is a Cosi across the street with a BIG lot, a Saxby's toward Ardmore with same, and this little gem in Bryn Mawr has enough room for about 8 cars.  Sometimes I drive by and laugh at what I see...that coffee MUST be good for people do deal with that parking situation.  Do yourself a favor and goto one of the Coffee shops with *real* parking.  Tell them Starbucks sent you!

Sadly, there is absolutely NOTHING they can do about it unless they buy some of Aqua's property to use as a lot.  Maybe AQUA should put up some dedicated meters for people to park for Starbucks...

In any case, please be careful parking here!  Or even driving by on Route 30 as sometimes cars are backing out of the entrance or just cued up waiting to get in.  Having to drive past this regularly, I'd say that is a solid reason to complain about the parking here!

If I go outside of my 7:05-7:15am window the baristas ask "regular morning" and lately "regular afternoon" - I'm addicted to the Low-Fat Mocha Frap in the afternoon!  I enjoy Komodo Dragon, Verona, Sumatra in the morning!  A frap or seasonal late in the afternoon!  The staff at this store are exceptional!  I have gone to other Starbucks and been very annoyed and disappointed!  I wish the Pumpkin Spice and Gingerbread Lates were on the menu 24/7!  If you're in Old City, check this place out...  It's cheaper than Old City Coffee and the staff are a lot more fun!

I am not that active on this site, however I decided to write a review after the terrible service we had this Starbucks location.   I have to add that I am a gold card member of Starbucks and  I have 2 pounds of their  Thanksgiving blend bought up for the holidays.

The lady in the drive thru was very  very short with us and  blurted out  they were no longer serving the Thanksgiving Blend for the season.   We then started to change our  order and before we could finish, the lady cut us off and told us our order total.  We asked for several items  such as muffins,  cookies, snowman cookies etc and  each item we requested they were out of them.  She never made suggestions as to what was available.   She acted as if she was doing us a favor  just by taking our order.     I rarely get too angry with a sales person however tonight I wanted to  to drive off without our order.  However we  followed thru and endured their slow service.   There will be no more stops at this location!

text:
I am out of carrots. I am out of sticks. This location has time and again proven that they are utterly incapable of producing the most basic of coffee drinks. I order an "iced coffee with cream, sugar, and a shot of espresso" every time. They repeat the order back correctly to me. Yet somehow they manage to omit the sweetener on an infuriatingly regular basis. 

I do not like the taste of their coffee, which is the whole reason I get an iced coffee with sugar so that it shocks my taste buds and I am blessed to drink a tasteless, sugary caffeinated drink. The lack of sugar makes the drink absolutely horrid and I cannot understand why I keep thinking they'll get it right this time. I keep giving them the benefit of the doubt but not anymore. Not again. 

I am dumbfounded. Bewildered. Utterly aghast at the surgeon like precision in which they dupe me into thinking they did it right this time. It is unfathomable and inexcusable and I will never make the mistake again.

text:
Definitely one of the bigger Starbucks locations in the area. There's a lot of open space and plenty of tables for meeting up with friends or getting work done. The back of the store does get a little dark though but there's tons of light towards the front.

The location is very well staffed and the staff is knowledgeable and friendly, even given how busy they are. The drive-thru seems especially busy at this location. Parking is also plentiful should you decide to stop inside.

text:
Here's the tea. I usually go to local nola cafe's just because I prefer their authenticity, charm, and local brews and grub. BUT I occasionally frequent SB's.

And I need these in a cafe: good coffee, vibe, coziness, privacy, tables, plugs, and friendly betches.  Here you get some of that.  Let me preface that I'm a local here- live right around the corner, and have been coming to this spot when it was Village Coffee for a long time and was among every one who was sad it closed.  Parking and layout remained the same.  I got the friendly baristas and good cafe au lait.  Loved the resurfaced modern, relaxed, clean feel.  It's spacious, and has restrooms and a coffee bar up&downstairs. Parking lot is tiny as it was before, but street parking isn't bad.  There are not that many tables inside, lots outside- love that, and upstairs balcony was one long buffet table. BUT no plugs whatsoever at any spot outside. Really?  The other personal issue was there were no tables alongside the windows (up or down) for privacy or a view, just two seaters. They had a few small tables inside upstairs along walkway=noisy and nosy. Then there was the f*&^#%$ music.  They were loudly playing emo punk music.  I'm talking Green Day,  Fall Out Boy, Paramore, etc.  Like that's great and all if you're a teenager from the 2000's and want to throw back.  It was obnoxious that I had to ask the barista to lower it down. Still loud and obnoxious.  Barista explained it's a preset IPOD at a preset volume. SB wants me to jam out to Paramore?! OH and when I couldn't punk rock anymore, I left around 3pm and the Paramore fan club arrived and crowded the upstairs like it was the after party with several kids who THOUGHT they were fly AF obnoxiously LOL'ing and singing out loud. Obv DJ Barista's friends. This coffee shop used to be frequented by professionals, and focused students when it was Village Coffee.  Now it just seems like a coffee bar.  Lastly, the WIFI. Literally had to connect to my mobile hotspot to finish what I was doing.  So what I thought would be a 4hr work day, was cut short to 1hr and I left in an emo mood. #rahhhhh

text:
Nice Starbucks location- convenient to a lot of things (City Park, Hynes school, etc.) and has everything you expect in a standard Starbucks (consistent coffee, efficient baristas, quick service, etc.)

Things that stand out about this particular Starbucks:

- lots of covered outdoor seating! On a nice day, this is a BIG plus- the fact that the outdoor area is covered makes a huge difference, too- you can go out there when it's pouring rain or hot direct sun and still stay comfortable. There's indoor seating too, including a counter along the windows if you're there on your own and just need a place to sit for a few minutes.

- Convenient parking- there are a few spots directly in front of the front door, and the neighborhood has lots of parking if the little lot is already full.

- Free Wi-fi....this is pretty common at coffee shops in town, but it's nice to know that yes, the wireless access is free here.

Obviously, Starbucks isn't a particularly local experience, but sometimes you just want a coffee drink that tastes the exact same as yesterday...so Starbucks it is. 

A slight bit of local interest is the "Katrina line" painted along the wall all around the outside of the coffee shop- this gives you an indication of how high the water was during the flood, just in case you were wondering about how much water this area of town got (hint- a lot!)

text:
You almost never see this location without a line in the drive-thru or a full cafe! This is THE place in the IRV where folks gather for business meetings, after-school study sessions, sewing circles and book clubs. You'll find a cornucopia of people here!

Wait times here are sometimes longer than other locations - sometimes suspiciously long when, after what seems like ten years, I only see one beverage handed over. And occasionally, a group of rowdy teenagers will interrupt gossip time with your best girlfriend. But outside of that, there's little here to frown upon. 

The place is cute and well-kept, and the baristas are friendly enough. Obviously the drinks and treats don't stand out from any other generic Starbucks. 

Certainly there are other locations I could travel to that would lessen my wait time, but it's really the convenience to my home that keeps me coming back time and time again.

text:
Changed their hours!!! DON'T go here now!!! This has been my favorite Starbucks (at least in Nashville) for a LONG time - and it's been especially cool since they remodeled their interior recently, so it's (in theory, I wouldn't really know) now an even better place to study and use your computer, etc. because it has a separate room just for that. And their service is definitely among the most efficient and friendly that you'll find at a Starbucks.

HOWEVER, just this past Saturday night after a show I arrived at 11:45 fully expecting the place to be open - as I've been there countless times RIGHT AT 11:59 and gotten a drink with no problem and plenty of politeness (it's not a tedious or complicated order). In fact they're often nice enough to let me in because they recognize me. This time though when I arrived they were completely dark and looked like they had been closed for hours (aside from the 4 or 5 people outside using their laptops). There was NO sign on the door - the hours sign that had been there was gone, though the suction cups were still in place. How helpful! I'll avoid making the obvious observation on the suction cups and how this situation su...is so incredibly frustrating.  I also had a treat receipt that went wasted because hey, I found out the same night that the West End location changed their hours too (although they're at least open half an hour later than this one on Friday and Saturday).

Anyway - come to find out (much later) this store has gone from being open until 11:30 through the week and midnight on Fri and Sat to 10:30 PM EVERY DAY!!! Unacceptable for such a prime location, especially one frequented by so many students and hospital employees. Even a MUCH LESS busy one in my city has better Fri & Sat hours than this. Ridiculous. Also, the shift manager who received the complaint about this really didn't seem to think it was a big deal in the least - not even the missing sign. Almost seemed happy about it. I only know about these hours because of the Starbucks website, by the way. To my knowledge they still aren't posted on site.  

****September 2013 update: open until 11 PM every night for now. Far as I know has been that way since at least June. Still seems to be very nice service, too. Still, not a fan of what happened with the hours/sign and still missing midnight!!

If you know me at all then you know I could care less about going to a Starbucks. I'll pretty much go with ANY other local coffee shop in the area as long as the java doesn't blow. Unfortuantely there aren't really any choices in this area...and Starbucks is the king of convenience.

text:
Hands down, this is the worst Starbucks location I have ever been to. Sadly, it is the only within a reasonable distance from my home so I continue to go. Every (and I mean EVERY) time I go the wait time is ridiculous whether I'm ordering a more intricate craft beverage or a tall blonde. This location gets backed up at all hours because the employees just really don't give a shit about the customers. I've gone inside to order multiple times to find one lone employee taking orders and literally no one making drinks regardless of how many people are in line. The employees have no sense of prioritizing the customer and are usually stocking, half assed cleaning, or socializing amongst themselves. The only semi-positive thing I can say about the staff is they are typically not rude, just oblivious. I cannot stress enough how much I despise this location and wish I had the willpower to stop throwing my money at this place. Oh, and the selection of whole bean coffee sucks if you're into that. 

Tl;dr - Avoid if possible.

text:
Meh, indeed .. 

The starbucks on the UofA campus is a disappointing experience.  It's not meant to be a place where people sit and drink coffee, like a regular starbucks joint.  But that doesn't particularly bother me.  It's the quality of coffee served there, is what I'm complaining about.

Working in the neighboring building, I have probably stopped here for a coffee, maybe 50 times and have been disappointed, maybe 35-40 times.  For some reason, the staff (invariably students at the university) don't know how to operate an espresso machine!  The cafe lattés taste like latté (the italian word latte means milk).  The cappucinos are invariably cold and generally, the service is quite poor with a big mess in the collection area and people's drinks always mixed up and confused. 

It's really like a big production movie with poor acting.  Disappointing indeed.  I guess if you were getting just a regular cup of coffee (drip coffee, i mean) .. you shouldn't be too disappointed because it's easier to stay within the norms and specifications that starbucks insist on, when loading the drip (for those who don't know, for the entire height of the drip machine, a predefined packet of coffee is used to load the top).  But apart from the drip - it's just a desperate option to go to.

text: So i was planning on going to Barnes & Noble on Dale Mabry, until i spotted this new Starbucks right next to the new Chipotle !! Whaaaat !? How awesome is that... if i get hungry after hours of studying i could just head next door !! So the parking area made me slightly claustrophobic... thank god i am a skilled driver (yeah right !!).  All kidding aside, If you came in from N Dale Mabry you get easy access to the angled parking spaces but if you come from Walnut St. you might encounter some difficulty. I don't know how they would have enough spaces for customers of both Chipotle and Starbucks on a busy day. I love the wall decorations and the different seating options they offer (from lounging spots, long counter height table, to 2 or 4 person seating). The inside looks bigger and more comfy than it appears to be from the outside. Initially, i was bummed about not having the long (non-counter height) table available to study, but the normal height 4 person seating was perfect as well. I sat with a gentleman who was also busy working on his laptop. The noise level was good for studying or working. What really made the difference were the employees that attended me. They did not seem like they were brown nosing, but like they were genuinely trying to help me out, especially when it had to do with the Starbucks Rewards program and Stars. Overall, i would definitely return (if i am ever in the area).

text:
If I could give zero stars for this store I would.  I had the unfortunate experience to witness a woman and her daughter being accosted by a staff member of Starbucks in Fishtown, Philadelphia.  I was in line waiting for a coffee when a women came in and asked her daughter what was taking so long to get her coffee.  It appeared the young lady was waiting well over 15 minutes for a large coffee.  The woman asked a staff member to check to see what the issue was since she was waiting so long.  The staff member immediately started verbally attacking the woman and shouting this is white privilege.  Another staff member came over who claimed to be a manager but did not or could not get the other staff member to stop verbally attacking the customer.  In very short order 4 staff members were all talking at the same time and getting louder and louder at the poor woman, who only wanted her daughter to get her paid for coffee and leave.

As to white privilege, I find it to be totally outrageous that a staff member would immediately start attacking a women with that claim, especially in Fishtown.  This area is not what you would call a white privilege area.  It is a medium to lower middle class neighborhood.  I find the entire encounter to be disgraceful and a very bad reflection on the Starbucks management team.  It is clear to me now that Starbucks willingly promotes this type of behaviour from their staff.

Suffice it to say, I will never visit this Starbucks again and have serious doubts as to whether or not I want to patronize any Starbucks again.  

Starbucks needs to wake up and smell the proviable coffee!  Who do they think is actually paying $7.00 for a cup of coffee!  It's certainly not the poor and downtrodden.

text:
I know what you're thinking ... "She's reviewing a Starbucks? Everybody knows what Starbucks is like!" Well, you don't know MY Starbucks, do ya? Yeah, that's right ... so shaddup! (please keep reading my reviews)

My Starbucks has more than just the slightly overpriced coffee, and the long counter at the window where you can sit and watch people go by, trying to creep them out with your staring. What, you don't do that?

My Starbucks has three (that's right, THREE) plushy seats in this little semi-private corner, and, get this -- a singing Starbucks girl. I call her that because I don't know her name. She's a very happy person, my singing Starbucks girl. And she doesn't care that everyone can hear her, she just sings whatever. Some ppl may find it annoying, but I like it ... it kind of just blends in with their music.

The coffee-shop also arranges their tables in a way so that not all the tables are smooshed together. I like that too, more privacy for each table. You can write or read whatever without feeling someone is peeking over your shoulder. Now if only they would lower their prices a bit, I could give them a full 5-star rating ... just kidding!

text:
Whomever was paid off to put in their review that the drive thru line was "the fastest in the nation" I encourage you to share what you're smoking with the rest of us still waiting in the drive thru line...

It doesnt matter what time you come here, 6am or 10pm, the line wraps the building. Go inside and you will be ignored for drive thru orders that are timed so it makes sense why the drive thru is always busy. 

Unless you get simple drinks here, your coffee will be incorrect. I used to drink a quad espresso and after chewing the ice block they provided, I switched to Nitro with sweet cream. Depending on the day, the sweet cream is not refrigerated and may curdle on the top of the cup. 

If you can, order ahead, risk your life trying to find a parking spot and get out of the parking lot and you should be in and out in 10 minutes and not 20 as compared to the drive thru.

text: Caffeine...now that's some old school ADD drug...

Someone was kind enough to spend their money buying me a Starbucks gift card so all I ask is that my drink be made correctly. Apparently, this location does not comprehend the value of a correctly made Starbucks drink. I ordered a tall blonde vanilla latte, hot. The first time, they made my latte iced. The second time, they made my latte with regular espresso instead of blonde espresso, and then had the audacity to get an attitude with me because I asked for my drink to be made correctly twice. 
I also wanted a reduced fat turkey bacon sandwich. I was told that they were out of breakfast sandwiches, but someone in line after me ordered a ham and cheese croissant. I politely brought it up to the barista that I thought they were out of breakfast sandwiches as per what I was told, to which she then replied that they were out of breakfast sandwiches so I asked if you're out of breakfast sandwiches why is the person behind me getting a ham and cheese croissant isn't it considered a breakfast sandwich? Then I asked, why is it that a reduced fat turkey bacon sandwich is considered a breakfast sandwich but the ham and cheese croissant is not, to which the barista reply that she doesn't know and that whether or not the turkey bacon sandwich is considered a breakfast sandwich depends on who you ask. Then she had the audacity to get an attitude with me yet again saying that regardless they were just out of the sandwich.
Hi definitely do not recommend this location of Starbucks because of the rude Barista's, and the horrible communication that takes place. Save your money and buy Starbucks coffee beans from your local grocers or go to one of the licensed locations like the one in the Hyatt regency on Perdido. They have great customer service, make great drinks, and have much better communication skills.

text:
This location is so hit and miss, and it's one of the most inconsistent stores I have been in. I live right around the corner so this is my local "didn't have time to make coffee before I had to leave for work" drive thru shop. Each time I come, the drinks I get taste different, a phenomenon that is so confusing because of the nature of what this nationwide chain is built on. I suggest management have some training refresher courses to up the standards for all of the employees. 
I've also had a few awkward experiences with some of the baristas' customer service skills. The baristas seem to be more friendly and talkative to each other, and usually ignore any kind of conversation with customers, even when I'm standing right there staring at them. 
I'll still frequent this place, only really out of occasional necessity, but I hope management reads this and takes some action to change the culter and vibe of the store and the employees.

text:
Probably one of the busiest Starbucks I've ever been to, but it was well worth it.

About the only negative I can see at this store is their lack of two public restrooms.  Oh, they have two restrooms, but one is reserved for employees only.

The atmosphere inside is terrific and kitschy like most locations and as packed as it was, we were still able to find seating for my wife, myself, and our niece.

Waited for several minutes for our mobile order and never heard my name or saw anything like what we ordered.  I approached the counter and asked if the app had selected the correct location.  The barista was shocked and told me that it was in fact a location far on the other side of town (Kino and I-10).  Turns out on our recent trips to Shreveport, LA and San Antonio, TX we had made stops at that SBucks, so the phone app defaulted to that store rather than the one we were standing in a few minutes earlier.

The barista asked to see the receipt, quickly jotted down our order, and promptly made our 3 drinks.  So amazing.  We thanked her profusely and then called the other store to let them know so they could gift the other drinks to some of their patrons.

Impressed to say the least.  We'll be back and I'll check the app a little more closely before ordering next time.

text:
It's exactly like every other Starbucks everywhere, except it has the additional excitement of having about half of their electrical outlets in the store, distributed randomly, just not work at all. There's no indication of which outlets work and which ones don't, and the employees have no fucking clue, so if you don't happen to be paying attention to which power icon appears in your taskbar, your computer may just suddenly shut down, losing all the work you've done since you last saved.

If they're too cheap to get their outlets fixed, they COULD spend three buck on this [ http://amzn.to/1QcZEN2 ], and then cover the non-working outlets, so that there's a reminder for customers that some don't work, plus an indication of which is which, but that would, like, require that someone test all the outlets, which would, like, be work, so they don't do it.

How much do you value that forty-five minutes of work you just lost because they're too fucking lazy to get their outlets fixed? I figure that five bucks plus a loss of almost an hour of work on my dissertation bumps the price to $$$$, hence my survey question answer.

Oh, and don't think they'll pull one of the five or six people chatting with their coworkers and ignoring the customers behind the counter onto the second register just because the line's out the door.

text:
I am always hesitant to write reviews for chain stores like Starbucks.  There isn't anyone coming to a Starbucks who is checking a Yelp review first.  Almost always, folks are headed to Starbucks because they need their legal fix of caffeine and know that Starbucks is a international consistent supplier.   

This Starbucks is no exception.  The staff is fantastic, I have always had great service here, and well made drinks.  I am a big fan of how Starbucks treats its staff (and have been for a very long time, they have treated several of my friends very well and earned my loyalty a long time ago).  It is nice to arrive in a shop and feel welcomed and like the staff are happy to see you. 

But, the layout of this Starbucks leaves something to be desired.  I like Starbucks as a workspace.  It is usually a great place to zone out and focus on my task at hand and still be around people.  All of the Starbucks around here seem to be moving towards a shared workspace model, with one large table with several chairs.  This encourages co-working at the table, and leaves more room for lounge chairs so people can be social.  There is a lack of open space, so they can't easily add more tables if they wanted to - so I guess they have done the best they can with with what they have to work.  It is a free standing store and can't really expand any further- it also has a drive through. 

I visited this Starbucks today because the Starbucks by campus on Central has remodeled and significantly reduced the aviable space.  They have added a co-working table there too.  At least this one still has more space for people to lounge and be social.  Central seems to have forced everything to take place at the co-working table. 

It is probably a choice from corporate Starbucks to reduce people loitering in their shops (which started before the recent troubles over race and kicking people out up north). 

Helpful tip- get a Starbucks card or app.  It is a pre-paid card and lets you discounts and free drinks.  If you are planning to loiter it is a great deal - because it makes the normally $.50 refills on ice coffee or tea free.

text:
I have a good friend who works downtown and when we want to get together for a quick coffee over his lunch break this has been our spot for about a year now. Unlike alot of places downtown it boasts a good sized free parking lot making it easy to get in and out of. Naturally however when you have a good location it draws a crowd and this one can get quite hectic especially in the mornings and at lunch time.

Seating is limited at this location (probably one of the smallest stand alone stores I have seen) which I don't think makes alot of sense for the amount of business they do. It's still a good location though,no matter how busy they get I have always gotten my drink quickly. Drinks are pretty standard no matter which Starbucks you go to and I have no issues with quality here. I come back here due to the warm personal attachment I get from being able to meet with a good friend however there are so many coffee shops downtown you wont miss out if you never make your way down to this one but there is no need to go running and screaming in the other direction either. Just your everyday run of the mill three star experience.

text:
I guess I visited on the worst day ever, because I couldn't wait for my experience at this Starbucks to end. I stopped by in the early evening for a sandwich and some quick blogging to pass an hour before I headed to my next adventure.

Everyone in the place was nice; I really want to emphasize that. The staff were very pleasant and eventually accommodating. But the overall service and atmosphere... ehhhh.

I ordered a tomato/cheese sandwich, an oatmeal cookie and a water to consume in-store. After I ordered, I put my bags down at a table and returned to the counter for my sandwich. I waited. And waited. And waited. One staff member was manning the drive-thru, while the other went... I don't know where. She returned eventually and fiddled around for a bit longer before noticing me, grabbing a sandwich and slipping it into a to-go sack. I looked down, confused, and asked if I could also have my water and cookie. She said that she didn't know I had wanted a cookie (even though I repeated it twice when ordering) and didn't charge me for it. I was silent, waiting for whatever would happen next, and she finally moved to the case, pulled out a cookie, put it in a to-go bag and rang it up. I thought that since it was her mistake and a minor expense, she should have simply given the cookie to me, but whatever, I paid. She then ran away before I could ask for my water again. I finally caught her and convinced her to hand over the glass of water she had apparently prepared for me before all of this rigamorole. I also requested a plate for my sandwich, which seemed a little puzzling to her, but she silently gave me one.

I finished off the sandwich, which wasn't the best, even by Starbucks standards, and got up to use the restroom. Unfortunately, there was no toilet paper. I headed back to the counter to ask for some, but the staff members had disappeared once again. I finally realized that the young man eating at one of the tables was a Starbucks employee, so I asked him for the paper. He told another staff member to get it. It took a while, but I eventually peed. Yay!

The cafe itself isn't the most conducive to working or the most comfortable. It's a small space, and the few tables were crammed so closely that you couldn't sit down without disturbing the chairs at the table behind you. There weren't many outlets, so if your laptop died, too bad, so sad.

Starbucks isn't my favorite place to eat or work, but generally, the chain is just fine. This location, though, doesn't do the brand any justice.

text:
While I'm not a Starbucks guy (too often burnt beans), this new drive thru sit down plant in the Village @ Newtown Shopping Center is very cool. Laid back, smaller footprint, friendly accommodating baristas, and decent breakfast profile, make for a pleasant interlude. Panoramic view of the ever expanding choices coming online in this primary commercial zone in Newtown. Can see Melt Shop, Factory Donuts, Naked Chocolate, Oishi, Nothing Bundt Cakes from my small deuce in the window. 

Here for a quick Saturday morning meeting, I enjoyed their oatmeal with blueberries, crannies, raisins and a touch of brown sugar (How come you dance so good?!). Perhaps, a little too much packaging for a health planet, but they do have choices. Even the Pike's Roast was more flavorful and less bitter than usual. 

Meeting went well, so this $$$bucks could become a strong house for getting together in private conversation. There is enough ambient noise from the polished concrete floor, high metal ceiling and sound system to mask any but the loudest voice.

text:
Hands down, Starbucks is my go-to coffee spot, and I've given many of the competitors a fair chance -- Coffee x-change, Peet's Coffee, Ike's Coffee, Cafe Luce.  Yet each time, I conclude that Starbucks offers a better cup of coffee and selection of food.

However this is the only location in Tucson where I've consistently seen the level of service fall, paired with poorly maintained restrooms (that is, when there isn't a line for the key or when they don't have the women's restroom blocked off for "storage"). 

I can no longer count the number of times where I've had my straw simply thrown on the drink hand-off counter rather than professionally placed on the counter or on top of the cup.  

They may as well start tossing my change at me through a hoop on the counter too.

It's also disappointing that after being a customer for over 6 years, they have yet to memorize my name or drink, unlike most of the other stores I'll visit.

It's pretty crazy that I have to settle for a coffee at Paradise in order to be appreciated after ordering a drink.

text:
It's a Starbucks.  New and shiny, but still a Starbucks.  They closed the other location, just a few blocks down next to Ceol, and they built this new location in the parking lot of the 1 Liberty Building.  I've heard that one of Starbucks' goals is to have every location have a drive thru, which makes sense since that's likely the most profitable transaction they can make.  

Parking is a bit strange in that they'd added spots specifically for Starbucks in the 1 Liberty lot, but no entrances for Starbucks on the east side of the building (which is where the lot is).  They've got a fake planter/barrier/divider thing to keep you from taking the shortest route to get to the entries on either the south or the west side of the building (which also happens to be right through the drive through lane).  Drive thru was doing solid business at 6:30 AM, probably 3 or 4 cars deep.

Tip: don't try to use the door on the north-east of the building - looks like it's stairs into the underground parking and is not connected to Starbucks.

The space is standard Starbucks - if you've been in any of the modern Starbucks you'd recognize it, and likely if you took someone in blindfolded, and had them identify the building, they'd know it was a Starbucks, so kudos to their branding and look and feel folks.  High ceilings set this place apart slightly, and there's more seating than the old location, with a mix of high tops, built-ins, etc.

I stopped in mainly since I wanted to try out the Reserve line of coffee, brewed on the Clover system.  The Reserve line is their new "premium" line, and the Clover is some fancy new brewing machine, single cup at a time that lets you program in exact water temperature and brew times, and uses a vacuum more than pressure.  It's a single cup system, and they grind and dose the beans after you order.  Interesting for a geek like me, but I think for most people it'll be seen as more of a novelty than worth the price premium.  The Reserve coffees ranged from I think 2.75 to 4.00 depending on the beans for a 12 oz.  Starbucks will probably have a bit more variety going through their doors with their Reserve line though, so that might make it worth while . . . 

Of course, the whole Reserve/Clover bit is Starbucks attempt to drive their bottom line up, trying to constantly grow as any publicly owned corporation must do to keep the stock market happy.  I think it is a better cup of joe than their Americano, albeit a bit expensive for what it is.  I think the cup I got this morning was good, but I also believe an Americano from the Hub or Bibo are excellent and comparable choices, for a buck or two less.

I won't be making any future attempts to stop in here explicitly, especially with the Hub on Cheney so close, but if I'm in the vicinity and in a hurry, the drive thru convenience could win out . . .

text:
Look for what it is, this Starbucks is great. Located on the first floor of the Marriott, this coffee house serves hotel guests, office workers, City Hall employees, SEPTA employees, and tourists down to see Reading Terminal market or City Hall. This all translates into foot traffic and high volume.
Short of adding a Wawa style self order kiosk, this place does all it can to maintain a high level of customer service. I've seen in 4 occasions free samples offered to entertain guests waiting for their orders to come up. That said, as mentioned by other yelpers, save yourself the time if you're in a rush and order on the app.
The drinks are usually served properly here. There is seating available however I wouldn't recommend planning on getting work done here. It's kind of cramped, and you're better served by visiting the Starbucks on Chestnut if you're planning on studying or working remotely. But if you just need a place to warm up or scratch your eyes open while catching up on emails on your phone, this venue can accommodate you.
For the volume of people, these guys do a great job of staying cheery and making customers feel at home. If you want in and out service use the app.

text:
I'm happy that there is finally a drive thru Starbucks. Great location and close to where I live which is awesome. I love the open space inside. There is a mix of high top tables with low tables and sofa seats to sit on. I like how everything is spread out including the food items. Everything is not jammed packed together. 

This location does need to hire more people. They need a person who handles customers ordering inside and one designated 1-2 people handling just drive thru. When I go though the drive thru it's not much of a wait but when I came inside there was a very long wait because the barista was handling inside and drive thru customers. Drive thru order where being served a lot faster then the customers inside waiting for their drinks/food. They were out of a ton of stuff because no one has yet to go through the boxes and open them to restock items. 

I like this Starbucks but they really need to hire additional staff especially because they have a drive thru. Being that this is a drive thru location they can expect more customers because of the convenience of not having to get out of the car. You need the staff to make sure both customers inside and outside are getting their order efficiently and timely.

WORSE CUSTOMER SERVICE !!
