text: I like Starbucks, and I love this location. Unfortunately the concept here is dragging its feet.  This is my Starbucks go-to, due to its convenience for me.  I was elated when it opened, and it deserves a trophy for often waking me up in the morning with its dark roast selections. Situated in the beautiful Matrix hotel, with its clean lined design and wrap around windows this coffeehouse has so much potential, in fact it could easily be the best Starbucks in Edmonton, if not one of the best in the entire Starbucks chain.... but only if it manages to fix itself. In the 6 years it has been open, the quality of service as well as the consistency of the beverages and the food have been steadily nosediving.  It's a staffing problem.  Though every barista behind the counter is exceptionally kind, friendly, and greets each patron with a genuine smile, the dizzying turn over of (evidently unexperienced) staff creates painfully slow service, and beverage and food inconsistencies. I feel for the management faced with this problem, and hope they can find, train, and retain the level of experienced staff that is required for such a high volume location. Again, this spot has so much potential, they have just completed a new Reno that seems to have tripled their seating, and this is THE place in Edmonton for celebrity sightings... from international recording artists, to big name Hollywood actors, the Matrix is where they all stay while passing through town on projects.  And this is where they grab their Starbucks coffee.

text:
I want to once again voice displeasure at this location. I've currently been waiting 20min. There's trash on the floor (& only one trash receptacle in the entire place - with the creamers) & boxes strewn everywhere. There were at least 15 cups lined up when I ordered. I understand bad days & maybe someone called in sick & it IS Sunday, but this is consistent. I stop here when it's on my way. 
I've heard rumor that the Cool Springs store is one of the higher volume stores in the region & I believe it. I have no idea why this store can't get it right.

Are you feeling lucky? This location is a total crap shoot. This morning, there was a friendly bearded guy at the window and they ROCKED it (someone promote that man). They had a line out to the street and I was out of there in under five minutes.  Other mornings, there's a REALLY chatty woman at the window who apparently has to milk the cow in the back before making my coffee and I feel like I'm going to die at the drive thru. 

The parking/lot situation is a total nightmare.  Whoever designed it should be fired.  You've got an extra entrance on the side that ruins everyone's day and the parking lot is only about five feet wide.  I would STRONGLY advise anyone to avoid parking during the morning rush. The space restriction ensures that you can only get out if there's no one at the drive thru, so you're pretty much stuck there until it either dies down or someone takes pity on your ass and lets you out.

So I'm giving them four stars because of my experience this morning. (Any other morning, I probably would have given them two.)

Still sitting here 15 minutes later waiting for my drink. Have watched countless cars drive through and get their orders. Very tired of the drive thru being given precedence over walk in traffic. Will not be returning.

text:
I really like Starbucks. It's convenient, reliable, and consistent... usually lol. I travel often and it's a great place for a small meal or a caffeinated beverage. Through my travels and daily life I've been to Starbucks all over the USA, literally. 22 states? And I must say this is the most unreliable, inconsistent, ball-dropping Starbucks I've ever been to. How do you screw an order up every single day? HOW DO MESS UP A STANDARD COLD BREW WITH SWEETENER - TWICE?

Recently I've been in the area helping family and working, so I have come here about 2-3 times a week for about a month. Of that I'm guaranteed to have at least - AT LEAST 2 botched orders. The line is normally 13-15 cars long (yes really) so you think "I know, I'll order ahead!"  WRONG lol. Best way to waste all your time and mess up your order. You actually wait longer! For real!

This has happened a few times.. you're standing in line, which is of course in front of a glass window.. and half the time customer service rep just helps whoever walks right up to the door. Really lol? YOU EVEN HAVE SPECIAL STARBUCKS TAPE OUTLINING THE WAITING LINE WITH BOXES FOR WHERE TO STAND. AND *YOU* CANT GET THAT RIGHT AS EMPLOYEES? Hahaha wow. 

Over the last month I've had:
-6 mobile orders out of an item, once 2 of 3
-sizes of the beverages incorrect 2x
-missed items from my order 
-COLD FOOD THAT SHOULD BE HOT 4X

text:
Be prepared to be underwhelmed and even slightly dissatisfied.  

I am without a doubt a very loyal Starbucks customer and have been for years. To the point that I definitely have my local to me locations but I stop by on a daily basis. This does not make me an expert, but it does make me a consistent consumer who is knowledgeable about Starbucks products and services. 

This location is located inside of the GSR hotel in Reno, and is frequented by predominantly hotel guests and staff. I can't imagine someone stopping by the hotel just to go to this specific Starbucks.  

The seating area is ridiculous. While it is open seating, it's located near the check in/out counters, as well as, gambling sections. It's just not a great spot/location overall.  

The service is close to horrendous, and the staff is just the same. 

I ordered two different drinks, both fairly easy to make, and I was the second person in line, with no one else behind me. My drinks took close to 25 minutes to be made. Yes, you read that correctly, 25 minutes for two drinks and no one else was waiting.

Once the drinks were ready, despite the fact that I was standing fairly close to the counter waiting, the barista put them on the counter and walked away. Did not call out my name, let me know that the drinks were mine versus maybe a mobile order, etc. then to top it off, both drinks were made incorrectly. Unfortunately, I did not discover this until I had already left the counter and was on my way outside of the hotel. No one wants to trek back into a hotel that is currently under construction, and frankly has become a hot mess to get in and out of. And let's face it, who wants to deal with unpleasant people when they are handling our food and or beverages?!  

Would  I stop by this location again? More than likely only under desperation.  My two stars are because if nothing else, the seating area and counters are clean.

text: Drove for about 4 hours and would stop because I wanted Starbucks.  Finally pulled over here, no one else in line and I asked if they had any cheese sandwiches.  I had looked, the Barista just said if it ain't in the case we don't have them.  Then I asked for what I always get and that is Frappuccino with some ground espresso beans in it  They refused to make it.  They said they could not make that drink at Starbucks.  So we got in the car, headed north on I65 stopped at the next Starbucks, the lady found us a cheese sandwich when we asked and she made my drink no problem.  Thanks Lebanon Starbucks for providing good service!

Went in at lunch, not too busy, several baristas working.  Asked first if they had any January cups left, barista looks around and says, no, I don't think so.  I asked if they would be in the front register area and he said, probably.  I ordered a venti cool lime refresher, tomato mozzarella panini and had picked up a box of cranberry bliss bar with a mark down of $8.43.  Rings me up, I pay with my app and he walks over to warm up panini.  I look at the receipt and the bliss bar rang up at $13.95.  I brought it to his attention and he said let me ask my manager.  Standing right there, she said, "Oh that ended yesterday."  There were several people waiting behind me by this time so I walked away, with the whatever attitude.  So I am waiting for my drink, and waiting, and waiting.  Finally I went up to the bar to inquire.  They forgot :(  So disappointing for this location (Stanford and McCarran) because I stop in here several times a week.

text:
I sometimes work late into the evening on my computer with returning emails, doing research, shopping and etc. I live within 2 blocks from this starbucks so sometimes I like to stop in for a cup of coffee to give me an extra jolt.  I recently stopped in to get a cup of coffee and a croissant. I asked the attendant to please heat up my croissant for 10 seconds.  She informed me that she already shut off the toaster because they sold all of their sandwiches so there was no need to keep it on.  I asked her what time it was, she informed me it was not yet 9 pm.  They close at 10 pm. I asked her if she had a microwave and she said no.  There were still cakes in the display which means they may still sell them.  I am confused of why they would shut off the toaster if they still had items to sell that the buyer may want to be heated up even though it's not a sandwich. Every time I go there, I always get a slice of pumpkin or banana bread or coffee cake to go with my coffee and they always ask me if I want it heated up and of course, I say yes. I don't guess I will be stopping in there later in the evening anymore if I can't have my food heated.  I ended up eating my blueberry croissant cold which was not very good.

Other than the complaint above,  the staff is always pleasant and efficient.

text:
I hate to say it because I've been to this location quite a bit over the last two years... It really declined about a year ago to maybe six months. They've improved a little since then, but not enough to make the rating average.

You have about a 50/50 shot of your drink being made correctly. Today I got whole milk instead of soy and unfortunately I have lactose issues. That's not the first time that's happened. Sometimes you order something cold and it comes back hot. This is definitely a location where you need to stress the details and make sure you mentioned everything you want. They've been out of stock a lot in the last year. I think it's due to COVID and the fact that they're a popular location.

The worst it's ever gotten is when I have to take the drinks from this location to Harding because they're just bad. There was one chai latte I swear was straight up just water and soy milk. 

Not my favorite location, but I think with the right team they have a lot of potential. Just wish I went in with certainty that I'd get the right thing every time (or almost, mistakes happen).

text:
I've been going to this Starbucks to study for months and now I'm upset that I now have to give them this review. On December 4, around  6:30-7:30pm while I was studying  a woman across the table from me was complaining about her charger and about how much money she makes. I continued to work and then the women begun to through racial slurs at me. I was confused and uncomfortable so I ignored her. She claims it was a Mexican who took it, then she said it was my cousin? And since I was ignoring her she said "Ya puta I am talking to you".. I was shocked. I put my earphones in and continued working.. THEN she started screaming at me saying the following 
"Mexicans are dirty"
"Mexicans are poor"
"Mexicans steal" and more. 

SHE WAS YELLING this at me and there were about 20-30 people in the building and they all LET IT HAPPEN. Not one single customer or employee attempted to stop her from harassing me. I was EXTREMELY uncomfortable and she kept yelling racial slurs at me and it was not until I asked her to stop but she kept going for about another minute then she left and was staring at me from outside the window. After I was done with my work I was reflecting on what had just occurred and I am not upset about the things that she was saying I was WAY MORE upset that NOBODY said anything, NEITHER the staff or customers. By staying quiet about this matter it makes all these people accomplices. I did not feel safe to go outside and I had to call somebody to meet me outside. I was very shocked because like I mentioned above, I am a regular at this coffee shop and their customer service is great but this time they disappointed me. Anyway, I called back the next morning to speak to the GM but the lady over the phone sounded like she annoyed with me for asking for when I can reach him so I decided I am going to be writing a letter. It really sucks because this a place I constantly study at but now I never want to go back and now I have to find a new place. I do not feel safe in a place where I can be easily harassed.

text:
Worst Starbucks experience, ever.  Even worse then stopping at a rest stop Starbucks - gasp.

I met a friend here on a Saturday morning, and it was chaos. We were getting on the road and wanted a quick pick-me-up before our long drive, but it would have been faster if I drove another shop and got it.

First, the person who took my order did not hand me my baked good (which normally happens when it's not heated). I asked about it after I paid, and he confirmed I had ordered it but didn't get the coffee cake for me. I thought maybe they would hand it to me with the drink, but that did not happen. In the meantime, a woman who ordered after me was handed her pastry at checkout, so apparently that does happen, just not for me. I ended up having to ask one of the other store personnel to get the coffee cake for me, after waiting for some time for my drink.

Then I went to put half-an-half in my coffee, and the debacle continued. There was no half-and-half out on the service station, so I inquired about it. I told a barista there a no carafe at all to begin with, yet she brought out a carton expecting to refill the carafe; but she couldn't, obviously. Instead of letting us use the half-and-half, she disappeared with it to look for a carafe, which took more than 5 minutes before she found one and then filled it before we could finally use it.

There was no order in the store and it was just Mayhem. I don't think the staff is well trained and could definitely use more supervision or additional training. I don't think I'll stop by here again.

text:
**This review is for the stand-alone Starbucks in the Delaware Welcome Center parking lot, not the one inside the food court.**

I have visited here twice and both times the staff was friendly and helpful.  The place is clean and there is seating.  There are two independent bathrooms.  The one I used was in pretty good condition especially when you take into consideration the rest stop locality.

Both times getting an iced green tea frap was easy and the results were delicious.  Both times I asked for no whip cream and both times it was taken care of easily.  There is a drive through so if you get food in the main building you can visit this Starbucks drive through before hitting the road again.

The staff here made a real difference for me.  I don't imagine they have many regulars and I completely appreciate that being friendly and helpful to a two time visitor holds very limited value.  Lets be honest, I'm not hopping on the road and driving up to Delaware for my next frap.  What I can do is tell folks about this location and encourage them to visit this location as they embark on the I-95 east coast driving experience.  

This stand alone Starbucks and its friendly staff is its own caffeinated oasis alone the I-95 corridor.

text:
I realize that this Starbucks isn't perfect. It's right around the corner from my house and I go once in a while to honestly just get a break from my house.  I think the universe wanted me to go today. 

It took a bit longer but I didn't mind because I needed the break from my toddler who just hit the attached to mom, scream until I get picked up phase. I quickly realized why the line was taking so long though. It was because the staff was taking their time making sure the customers were truly taken care of. I got to the order stand and completely spaced what I wanted and forgot the name of the main thing I came for. I apologized and said I forgot but my brain is tired and barely functioning. The reply I got was just what I needed. "Take your time. I have three kids and I get it." She then suggested I get an extra shot which I honestly didn't think even know you could do. I grabbed my perfectly done order and headed home. 

Then I saw the note. Small but not gonna lie, I cried a little.  I cannot stress enough how much I needed that and how great the customer service was. So bravo to this staff for taking the time in a crazy world to make one tired mom's day.

text:
One of my favorite area Starbucks - it's got a great modern interior, plenty of seating, and it moves drinks quickly.

If coming to relax and chill with your drink is the name of your game, then this is the perfect place - there is an area of 6 super-comfy Restoration Hardware style chairs at the front of the store. I always seem to be able to grab one, but I typically come at off-peak times (mid-afternoon; but come to think of it, is there really ever any particular peak times at a coffee house outside of morning rush?).

This location always has some interesting tunes to play in the background, that aren't overwhelming to studying or conversing.

I haven't had any reason to complain about the drinks that the baristas have pumped out, thus far.

The ordering area has a food display case that is usually stocked with most every particular thing that the Starbucks company offers. It probably wouldn't take you long to request something in the case, after perusing it!

Also, there is more than plenty enough parking here; if that's something you're worried about.

text:
This is probably the slowest Starbucks I've ever been to. It looked like the 8 or so employees were just shuffling around trading places and only one of them was actually making drinks. I walked in and there was NO LINE and only 3 cars in the drive thru yet it still took 5 minutes of me standing directly in front of the register to have my order taken and 5 more minutes to get my drink. I ordered an iced chai! Literally scoop ice, pour milk in, pour chai concentrate, serve...and THAT took 10 minutes. 

Adding to the frustration is the fact that my nearly $5 "tall" (small) drink tasted like a glass of chai-flavored milk because there wasn't enough TEA in it. This will be my first and last trip to this Starbucks. I'll go to a gas station or drive to the Kahwa truck before I walk over to this place again.

Not sure what's worse: Bad customer service or the cashier intentionally not comprehending a simple order just for the sake of of being obstinate. 

You know the order! An order that's placed at a Starbucks a hundred times a day! A Chai Latte! THAT should be easy!

This was my first and last time at this Starbucks. 

Here's how I order -every time-: "a Venti chai tea latte with soy and light whip". Easy, right? Done.

But then, when my companion orders HER chai latte BUT with a shot of caramel, cashier says, "We don't have soy, it's 1%, 2%, or coconut milk". Wait. Whaaaaa?

So let's confuse the issue, you're all of a sudden not offering soy milk? My companion questioned it, but cashier claims ignorance. My companion, seemingly not having the choice to order her drink as she likes it, is disgusted with the barista being obstinate, then forced to order the coconut milk. We were both shaking our heads by this time, and both pretty weirded out by the barista.

Of course, as anticipated, it tasted terrible. FYI, coconut milk, mixed with a caramel shot, does not go well with Chai Tea Lattes. 

We asked them to remake it.

The part that really blew me away was the attitude I got because we asked them to re-make my companion's drink. I had to humor them! I had to laugh along with them while they were being ridiculous about the one drink. Because if I didn't, bad things might have happened with that newly-made drink. And yet MY drink was perfect. Ugh!

text:
I understand how swamped the Resturant industry can get at times, I'm a server and bartender myself. I completely get that Starbucks gets crowded and the employees work very hard. However the over populated crowd, rush or in the weeds as we call it was not going on when I went into this Starbucks. I asked for a green tea iced latte and the first thing I noticed is my name on another guys cup as he left. I thought to my self, great they probably won't remember  my order now. They did remember but handed me my drink with all the green tea powder floating on top of the drink. 

I've ordered this drink probably hundreds of times and it's never been server to me in such a sloppy manner. I experienced terrible customer service when I asked them to mix it up they simply said they can't put milk in a blender. Clearly they didn't understand what I was asking because I didn't want it blended, just mixed in together. 

The Latino man with the goatee completely ignored me and said I'll give you a cup to skate it in. "Old school way." I said.... Ok. Looking around as if I was on the show punked. Is this a joke?? 

I had to mix my drink myself. . . Great customer montecito Starbucks. You get a gold star! 

But you don't get my business anymore. Since I had to mix my own drink I'll just do it at home from now on. 

Not at all sorry during the mixing process the drink spilt on the counter. 

So displeased with their lack of customer service. and attention to detail.

text:
I've never been a fan of Starbucks.  I don't understand why people insist on overpaying for an over-roasted cup of coffee?  It just doesn't make sense to me.  While the Very Berry Hibiscus refresher and most of the smoothies are okay, I can't justify spending the money there on a regular basis.

Inside it's your run of the mill Starbucks, with nothing negative or substantially positive added to the overall experience.  I've had friends who work at this location ... so I guess that's one redeeming quality.   It's fairly quiet and offers free Wi-Fi, so there's that.  

In a pinch you could do worse than Starbucks, but I'd recommend heading a block over to the Monon Coffee Company and getting a better cup of coffee or one of their incredible teas.  You'll save a little money on your coffee and get a way better experience.

text:
Even in an Airport I will not concede to subpar coffee, I will find the closest Starbucks and even if it's out of the way I will make a trip to get my morning coffee.  

My flight was leaving at 7am and I was definitely needing my coffee once I made it through security.  I was flying out of terminal D and this Starbucks was a short walk and was between terminals B and C.

text: This Starbucks rates a review because, for better or worse, it has character. During the day it's so packed with laptop users you'd swear you entered a tech start-up's unorthodox work commune were it not for the baristas' familiar outfits. As dusk fades, it attracts a fairly regular crowd of Tampa's finest minimum wage earners outside while the inside seems a bit like a study hall. This is one of those rare Starbucks that seems to approximate the Platonic coffee house, with regulars either spending all day exploiting the free wireless connection alongside a cuppa joe or just living the slightly overpriced Bohemian life as they dread the limbo between when this once 24hr Sbux closes and when they can return to what has clearly become their neighborhood haven.

text:
Some say that Starbux is Starbux no matter where you go but I have to disagree. Out of the three or so locations in the "greater Northeast," this is definitely one I prefer. They seem to have the least waiting time (if you're getting something inside), they have enough of the outside seating (3 tables for them and 3 tables for the mexican place next door which are shared), wifi always works gret (I go there a lot when I am working from home) the staff is always smiling and they actually don't burn their coffee as much as some of the other locations. 

Now, some things could be a little better:
1. The wait at the drive-thru is often much longer than I anticipate and, for that matter, prefer... I am not sure what the cause is but once, I waited for 18 minutes while there were only 2 cars in front of me)
2. They could do a better job cleaning outside after those very nice people who neglect to throw out their trash

text: So, normally I would have returned a starbucks that was wrong but I had multiple drinks and I didnt notice this mistake. This has happened at this starbucks on more than one occasion now. It hard enough to order a drink at starbucks, but it's more frustrating to have to try and explain how it's made. For a few months now starbucks has had the pumpkin cream cold brew and the irish cream cold brew. They were very good. I liked the way they were made. Black coffee with cream on the top. I've tried to explain to this location that I understand that the irish cream and pumpkin cream is gone, I just want a sweet cream cole brew or add a pump of vanilla to the cream. They just dont get it and basically give me iced coffee with milk and flavor mixed in, which is not what I want. I dont know how to order this drink. If I wanted an iced vanilla latte I would have ordered it. I asked for an irish cream cold brew with different flavoring. Now I'm stuck with this drink, with probably a ton of sugar. Is it so much to ask for some cold brew with cream on top??
